job.job_description:
WHAT MAKES US A GREAT PLACE TO WORK


We are proud to be consistently recognized as one of the world’s best places to work. We are currently the top ranked consulting firm on Glassdoor’s Best Places to Work  list and have earned the #1 overall spot a record seven times. Extraordinary teams are at the heart of our business strategy, but these don’t happen by chance. They require intentional focus on bringing together a broad set of backgrounds, cultures, experiences, perspectives, and skills in a supportive and inclusive work environment. We hire people with exceptional talent and create an environment in which every individual can thrive professionally and personally.   


WHO YOU’LL WORK WITH
You’ll join Bain’s Tech Solutions BBS team, a trusted advisor to the business on technology. Our team partners across functions to understand core business processes, provide consultation to solve challenges, and ensure business value is defined, realized, and recognized. We focus on cross-functional challenges and end-to-end processes, delivering a world-class, customer-centric technology experience that underpins Bain’s strategy to out-team, out-innovate, and out-grow as we play for #1.

 

 

WHERE YOU’LL FIT WITHIN THE TEAM
As a Senior Analyst, TSG Product Management, you’ll play a critical role in Bain’s global implementation of Salesforce.com. You’ll translate customer needs into an actionable backlog, then partner with Global Commercial Operations, stakeholders, and technical teams to ensure successful product delivery.

 

 

WHAT YOU’LL DO

Drive change requests and stakeholder engagement (50%)

  • Understand key business objectives and product strategy from leadership
  • Elicit requirements for enhancements, new functionality, and integrations
  • Identify, track, and analyze key metrics to drive actionable insights
  • Support stakeholder communication, pilots, and training
  • Troubleshoot issues, identify root causes, and translate into user stories

Product delivery (40%)

  • Participate in Agile ceremonies for this product
  • Transform requirements into user stories with technical detail and supporting materials
  • Collaborate with technical teams and vendors to optimize Bain’s experience
  • Test, validate, and provide input on work in progress
  • Manage user provisioning and deprovisioning, ensuring accuracy and compliance
  • Support release management activities and communications

Domain knowledge (10%)

  • Build and maintain expertise in Bain’s internal processes and external market trends

 

 

ABOUT YOU

  • Bachelor’s degree in Business, Information Systems, Computer Science, or equivalent experience
  • 2+ years of experience as a product owner, business analyst, or similar role
  • Proficient with Agile methodologies
  • Strong communication and interpersonal skills with a customer-first mindset
  • Skilled in defining, documenting, and breaking down requirements
  • Collaborative thought partner who works well across teams
  • Experience with Salesforce.com Sales Cloud required; certification preferred (Admin, Business Analyst)
  • Willingness to travel if needed

 


 

U.S. COMPENSATION INFORMATION

Compensation for this role includes base salary, annual discretionary performance bonus, 401(k) plan with an annual employer contribution based on years of service, and Bain’s best-in-class benefits package.

 

Some local governments in the United States require a good-faith, reasonable salary range be included in job postings for open roles. The estimated annualized compensation for this role is as follows:

 

  • In Georgia state, the good-faith, reasonable annualized full-time salary range for this role is between $92,500 - $110,500; placement within this range will vary based on several factors including, but not limited to, experience, education, licensure/certifications, training, and skill level.

 

  • In Illinois, the good-faith, reasonable annualized full-time salary range for this role is between $101,000 - $121,500; placement within this range will vary based on several factors including, but not limited to, experience, education, licensure/certifications, training, and skill level.

 

  • In Massachusetts, the good-faith, reasonable annualized full-time salary range for this role is between $106,000 - $127,000; placement within this range will vary based on several factors including, but not limited to, experience, education, licensure/certifications, training, and skill level.

 

  • In Texas, the good-faith, reasonable annualized full-time salary range for this role is between $96,500 - $116,000; placement within this range will vary based on several factors including, but not limited to, experience, education, licensure/certifications, training, and skill level.

 

Additional compensation may include:

  • Annual discretionary performance bonus
  • 4.5% 401(k) company contribution, increasing after 3 years and fully vested upon start
  • Full coverage of individual medical, dental, and vision premiums
  • Generous paid time off, including parental leave and holidays
  • Paid life and long-term disability insurance
  • Annual fitness reimbursement

 

MASSACHUSETTS EMPLOYMENT NOTICE

It is unlawful in Massachusetts to require or administer a lie detector test as a condition of employment or continued employment. An employer who violates this law shall be subject to criminal penalties and civil liability.

Bain & Company is a global consultancy that helps the world’s most ambitious change makers define the future.
Across 65 cities in 40 countries, we work alongside our clients as one team with a shared ambition to achieve extraordinary results, outperform the competition, and redefine industries. Since our founding in 1973, we have measured our success by the success of our clients, and we proudly maintain the highest level of client
job.job_description:
WHAT MAKES US A GREAT PLACE TO WORK

We are proud to be consistently recognized as one of the world’s best places to work. We are currently the top ranked consulting firm on Glassdoor Best Place to Work list and have earned the #1 overall spot a record seven times.
 Extraordinary teams are at the heart of our business strategy, but these don’t happen by chance. They require intentional focus on bringing together a broad set of backgrounds, cultures, experiences, perspectives, and skills in a supportive and inclusive work environment. We hire people with exceptional talent and create an environment in which every individual can thrive professionally and personally.

WHO YOU’LL WORK WITH

About the Commercial Excellence practice

Bain’s B2B Commercial Excellence Practice works with its clients to develop a clear GTM strategy including building an in-depth understanding of market opportunity, developing effective GTM sales coverage and capacity models to attack that opportunity, and creating an industrialized sales execution capability to ensure commercial success. Coro’s software and data solutions is an innovative and increasingly critical part of this approach to ensure success for our end-customers. To learn more about Coro, visit the website: https://www.coro.io/.

About CoroSM

The firm’s CoroSM business unit brings together Bain’s proprietary suite of software as a service (SaaS) and data as a service (DaaS) tools, including cloud-based software, online capability assessments and advanced analytics, focused on enabling Commercial Excellence for all B2B companies. By developing proprietary solutions across a range of go-to-market challenges – such as opportunity mapping, indirect channel growth and profitability analysis – CoroSM has created a set of highly differentiated solutions which enable our teams to deliver better insights to our clients.

WHERE YOU’LL FIT WITHIN THE TEAM

Bain & Company is seeking to recruit a Coro Consultant with advanced analytics expertise to join the firm’s Commercial Excellence Coro team in the Americas. The Coro Consultant will join the Coro delivery team to deploy Coro solutions, bringing deep data science, analytics, and modeling capabilities to generate actionable insights and drive value for clients. This is a client-facing role that involves deep interactions with clients as well as working deeply with the Bain case teams and commercial excellence solutions. As part of GTM strategy support, Bain’s Coro team leverages data science to build an in-depth, data-driven view of market opportunities, such as in our MoneyMap™ solution. The Coro Consultant will support and enhance these solutions through data matching, large-scale data aggregation, and advanced analytics, tuning Coro’s tools to meet client needs. In addition, they may also deploy other Coro tools like Partner Sonar or Profit Cube. 

The ideal candidate will have experience deploying CRM and data analytics solutions, as well as strong skills in managing and manipulating large data sets using tools like SQL and Python. Experience as a consultant focused on commercial excellence and data science will be valuable, with a track record of identifying issues, developing hypotheses, executing analyses, and guiding clients toward actionable insights. The person will need to interface with the Coro Data Science manager, consulting team manager, the Commercial Excellence center of expertise (currently based out of India and Mexico) as well as Bain partners and experts to deliver value through the end-client engagements and capture learnings and recommended changes for ongoing process and product improvements. The role also provides opportunities to work with Coro product managers to ideate improvements and new features, as well as with Practice / Product leaders to design the optimal approach to product delivery. The individual would be deployed across ~1–3 different client engagements at any given time with support from the Coro delivery LT. In each of these deployments, they will leverage the Coro solutions specifically developed (or being developed) for Commercial Excellence engagements.

WHAT YOU’LL DO

Client-facing

  • Work with case team manager to understand the scope of work; provide relevant advice on Commercial Excellence, delivery model, applicable (internal and 3rd party) software tools, and bespoke data analysis using advanced analytics, machine learning, and GenAI
  • Project manage market opportunity definition workstreams including defining deliverables, setting timelines, and delivering high-quality output
  • Liaise with client map to understand client systems, data sources/structures relevant for Commercial Excellence work
  • Assist in the facilitation of key workshops and interviews with clients to develop hypotheses and build buy-in related to Commercial Excellence work
  • Support, and lead, on-shore / off-shore teams to perform predefined, as well as custom, analyses, including advanced analytics work deploying state of the art tools (ML algorithms, GenAI analysis of large datasets, etc.)
  • Support client training (in conjunction with consulting manager, other case team members, and Next-Gen consultants) in use of relevant Commercial Excellence software tools and advise case teams on how to use full tool capabilities, as needed
  • Manage and support set-up and configuration process of Coro solutions, working with case teams, as needed. This may include
      • Supporting the scoping of tool usage within case context, and identifying visuals to support key questions
      • Serving as first-line support during tool deployment phase for user queries and escalates / delegates where appropriate
      • Supporting pre-sales engagements to help understand client challenges and recommend optimal approach and configuration
  • Co-locate with the Bain case team during the case as required for case needs
  • Support customers beyond the initial engagement in customer success motions

Capability building

  • Become an internal expert in GTM strategy and data science within the Commercial Excellence practice
  • Work closely with Coro product managers and leaders to deepen Bain’s ability to serve clients through data science and AI-enhanced Coro solutions
  • Drive demand for Coro’s data science and AI-driven solutions, suggesting analytics and machine learning enhancements to expand the Coro suite’s capabilities
  • Work with the Coro team to develop and refine training manuals, user guides, and other training materials (e.g., videos) 

Team engagement

  • Communicate effectively with case team concerning the work, the client relationship, and any Coro cadence/timelines
  • Exert strong positive influence and professional development for team members by leveraging content expertise to provide coaching on problem-solving, work-planning, data science techniques, etc. as applicable
  • Constructively engage in mutual feedback process with supervisor and team members
  • Identify opportunities to support the growth and scaling up of the Coro business unit including planning, recruiting, developing people, training, etc. as possible 

Technology product awareness and adoption

  • Develop deep expertise in the Coro solution toolkit and advise consulting teams on features and capabilities, especially analytics and machine learning components
  • Assist the Coro team in collecting, aggregating, and synthesizing user feedback related to the Coro suite of software tools; work with the Coro team in developing and prioritizing software enhancements and roadmap
  • Deliver demos to Bain and client stakeholders for Coro tools and other Commercial Excellence products as required
  • Provide new / pipeline case teams with relevant examples from previous users and other proposal collateral (e.g., dashboard screenshots, training materials, executive presentations)

ABOUT YOU

This role requires a minimum of three days per week working together in person, either at a client location or at your Bain home office. Travel is required. This position requires regional travel away from your home office/primary working location. Travel frequency and destinations will vary based on project needs. 

  • BA or equivalent
  • Distinct commercial excellence experience, ideally experience running complex analytics and working with large datasets, as well as business consulting experience
  • Proficiency in data science and analytics tools (e.g., Python, SQL, R), visualization platforms (e.g., Tableau), and experience building RAG-based and agentic AI systems
  • We are looking for candidates that fit at least one of the below criteria:
    • 1+ year/s of experience as a Bain consultant  or Bain AAG Client Lead
    • 4+ years of industry level experience in a data science or commercial operations role (MBA preferred in this case)
  • Ability to quickly grasp and apply complex concepts
  • Strong abilities in problem definition and problem-solving
  • Excellent analytical capabilities and experience using complex modeling and analysis to inform key business decisions
  • Excellent oral, written, and PowerPoint communication skills
  • Proven ability to work effectively, and manage others in diverse and distributed teams
  • Ability to work well with all levels within Bain and at client
  • Ability to lead interactions with senior stakeholders (at Bain or Client)
  • Comfort working independently and/or remotely from supervisors in a fast-paced environment
  • Willingness to travel to client locations (expected 20-40%, potential peak of 60%)

Preferred:

  • MBA, or PhD in a technical field. Advanced understanding of data science methodologies, including machine learning and modeling, with a strong analytical background
  • Formal Agile/SCRUM certification. Experience supporting deployment of SaaS solutions with customers/clients or similar roles. Experience in software customer success roles. Experience in technology product management and project management.

 

U.S. Compensation Information 

Compensation for this role includes base salary, annual discretionary performance bonus, 401(k) plan with an annual employer contribution based on years of service and Bain’s best in class benefits package (details listed below).

Some local governments in the United States require a good-faith, reasonable salary range to be included in job postings for open roles. The estimated annualized compensation for this role is as follows:

  • In New York, California, Texas, Massachusetts, Illinois, and Georgia, the good-faith, reasonable annualized full-time salary range for this role is between $128,500-$171,500; placement within this range will vary based on several factors including, but not limited to experience, education, licensure/certifications, training and skill level  
  • For all other locations, the good-faith, reasonable annualized full-time salary range for this role is commensurate with competitive geographic market rates for this role and will vary based on several factors including, but not limited to experience, education, licensure/certifications, training and skill level  
  • Annual discretionary performance bonus  
    • This role may also be eligible for other elements of discretionary compensation  
  • 4.5% 401(k) company contribution, which increases after 3 years of service and is 100% vested upon start date 

Bain & Company's comprehensive benefits and wellness program is designed to help employees achieve personal independence, protection and stability in the areas most important to you and your family. 

Bain pays 100% individual employee premiums for medical, dental and vision programs, offering one of the most comprehensive medical plans for employees without impacting your paycheck  

  • Generous paid time off, including parental leave, sick leave and paid holidays 
  • Fully vested 401(k) company contribution 
  • Paid Life and Long-Term Disability insurance 
  • Annual fitness reimbursements 

It is unlawful in Massachusetts to require or administer a lie detector test as a condition of employment or continued employment. An employer who violates this law shall be subject to criminal penalties and civil liability.
job.title: Expert Consultant, Data Sciences, Coro

Across 65 cities in 40 countries, we work alongside our clients as one team with a shared ambition to achieve extraordinary results, outperform the competition, and redefine industries. Since our founding in 1973, we have measured our success by the success of our clients, and we proudly maintain the highest level of client
job.job_description:
WHAT MAKES US A GREAT PLACE TO WORK

We are proud to be consistently recognized as one of the world’s best places to work. We are currently the top ranked consulting firm on Glassdoor’s Best Places to Work list and have earned the #1 overall spot a record seven times.

 

Extraordinary teams are at the heart of our business strategy, but these don’t happen by chance. They require intentional focus on bringing together a broad set of backgrounds, cultures, experiences, perspectives, and skills in a supportive and inclusive work environment. We hire people with exceptional talent and create an environment in which every individual can thrive professionally and personally.


WHO YOU’LL WORK WITH

FRWD is Bain & Company’s marketing acceleration capability, a 200-person community of expert practitioners who partner with clients (and their agencies) to drive meaningful business results and transformation. We are:

  • Expert practitioners sourced from some of the world’s leading in-house marketing teams, agencies and tech platforms
  • Focused on delivering actionable insights with a passion for results
  • Define not only What to do and Why but also How – rooted in real marketplace expertise

FRWD’s work revolves around solving Bain clients’ toughest marketing challenges. Often, this tends to take a few common forms:

  • Marketing diagnostic – Develop a data-driven perspective of marketing performance of a company, brand or asset, along with prioritized opportunities to drive value
  • Marketing acceleration – Help clients drive transformational change, new capabilities and learning to create sustainable competitive advantage. 
  • E-commerce acceleration – Improve operational value capture in key commerce channels with 3P retailers and DTC. 
  • Operating model – Set up the marketing organization for success (including MarTech and data strategy); build capabilities and ways-of-working.
  • Due diligence – Partner with private equity fund clients to assess the marketing capabilities of target assets, and help build value creation plans.

WHERE YOU’LL FIT WITHIN THE TEAM

A FRWD Expert Consultant is a seasoned practitioner and emerging marketing expert, driving key workstreams within integrated Bain casework. Expert Consultants work hand-in-hand with client teams and cross-functional peers from across the Bain ecosystem. They have a proven track record of leveraging in-platform experience, data, and analysis to generate insights for clients and contribute to overall case results. They partner effectively with client teams and can clearly explain the impact and importance of their workstream on the broader marketing strategy and connect marketing insights to the broader business goals.

WHAT YOU’LL DO

The Expert Consultant role at FRWD centers around a few key functions, specifically:

Problem solving and case delivery

  • Drive key workstreams (with support from leadership team) and deliver results to meet client expectations and build client promoters
  • Partner with generalist Bain consultants in a blended team environment, bringing to bear the best of all skillsets and expertise
  • Demonstrate their reputation as a seasoned marketing practitioner with proficiency in distinct areas of expertise (e.g., industries, channels, platforms, products, tools, etc.)
  • Lead a wide range of analyses and consistently deliver outputs and materials that are insightful and free of errors
  • Leverage proficiency in-platform (digital ad, E-commerce, etc.) to gather data, generate insights, and develop testing plans
  • Bring knowledge and expertise at the forefront of marketing technology and tools (GenAI, attribution and measurement, MarTech/AdTech, data)

Client communication and results

  • Leverage expertise and interpersonal skills to build trusting relationships with clients and their agencies and work collaboratively with all parties
  • Communicate clearly and consistently with team and clients to share insights and ensure alignment on workplan
  • Bridge between specialized expertise and client business challenges, transforming marketing insights into actionable strategic recommendations that drive measurable impact

Extraordinary teaming

  • Maintain a consistently positive and at-cause attitude, and contribute to day-to-day work across the case team
  • Actively seek out and act upon feedback from team, peers, and leaders in the business
  • Share expertise and best practices with team members across the FRWD ecosystem and across the broader Bain ecosystem
  • Contribute to FRWD strategy and operations (e.g., sponsor internal initiatives, support the recruiting and hiring process, etc.)


ABOUT YOU

  • 5+ years of relevant industry experience in marketing roles (e.g., agency, brand, in-house, or technology platform) with clear leadership experience and consistent trajectory 
  • Bachelor’s degree required; MBA not required but preferred
  • Exceptional strategic thinking and analytical skills to solve complex problems

    Experience contributing to high performing, cross-functional, integrated teams

  • Ability to solve tough problems effectively and efficiently, breaking them down into pieces and taking a hypothesis-driven approach

  • Demonstrated proficiency in specific marketing domain(s), recognized by peers and superiors

  • Strong expertise in the digital media landscape, including media partners/products and industry best practices

  • Exceptional planning and organizational skills, ability to meet deadlines and work with cross-functional teams

  • Strong verbal and written communication skills

  • Positive, at-cause attitude

  • Willingness and ability to travel as needed

In addition to broad marketing expertise, deeper expertise across a select few areas of focus and growth for FRWD, including:

  • E-commerce and retail media
  • Marketing measurement and analytics
  • Marketing technology
  • GenAI in marketing (e.g., agentic search)
  • B2B marketing
  • Client services experience (from consulting, agency, or other similar contexts)
  • Certification in some subset of relevant digital channels: Meta Blueprint, Google Ads, Amazon Ads, The Trade Desk Trading Academy, Adobe Marketing Platform


ABOUT US

Bain & Company is a global consultancy that helps the world’s most ambitious change makers define the future. Across 61 offices in 38 countries, we work alongside our clients as one team with a shared ambition to achieve extraordinary results, outperform the competition, and redefine industries.

Our vibrant ecosystem of digital innovators complements our tailored, integrated expertise to deliver better, faster, and more enduring outcomes. We’ve committed $2 billion in pro bono services over the next decade to support organizations tackling today’s urgent challenges.

We’re proud to be recognized with a platinum rating from EcoVadis, placing us in the top 1% of consulting firms for environmental, social, and ethical performance. Since 1973, we’ve measured success by the success of our clients—and we continue to lead the industry in client advocacy.

U.S. Compensation information: 

Compensation for this role includes base salary, annual discretionary performance bonus, 401(k) plan with an annual employer contribution based on years of service and Bain’s best in class benefits package (details listed below). Some local governments in the United States require a good-faith, reasonable salary range be included in job postings for open roles. The estimated annualized compensation for this role is as follows: 

  • For New York, the good-faith, reasonable annualized full-time salary range for this role is between $122,000-146,500. Placement within this range will vary based on several factors including, but not limited to experience, education, licensure/certifications, training and skill level.
  • For Illinois, the good-faith, reasonable annualized full-time salary range for this role is between $107,000-128,500. Placement within this range will vary based on several factors including, but not limited to experience, education, licensure/certifications, training and skill level.
  • For Minnesota, the good-faith, reasonable annualized full-time salary range for this role is between $97,000-117,000. Placement within this range will vary based on several factors including, but not limited to experience, education, licensure/certifications, training and skill level.

Additional compensation may include:

  • Annual discretionary performance bonus
  • 4.5% 401(k) company contribution, increasing after 3 years and fully vested upon start
  • Full coverage of individual medical, dental, and vision premiums
  • Generous paid time off, including parental leave and holidays
  • Paid life and long-term disability insurance
  • Annual fitness reimbursement

Bain & Company's comprehensive benefits and wellness program is designed to help employees achieve personal independence, protection and stability in the areas most important to you and your family.

  • Bain pays 100% individual employee premiums for medical, dental and vision programs, offering one of the most comprehensive medical plans for employees without impacting your paycheck
  • Generous paid time off, including parental leave, sick leave and paid holidays
  • Fully vested 401(k) company contribution
  • Paid Life and Long-Term Disability insurance
  • Annual fitness reimbursements

  • Assist the Coro team in collecting, aggregating, and synthesizing user feedback related to the Coro suite of software tools; work with the Coro team in developing and prioritizing software enhancements and roadmap
  • Deliver demos to Bain and client stakeholders for Coro tools and other Commercial Excellence products as required
  • Provide new / pipeline case teams with relevant examples from previous users and other proposal collateral (e.g., dashboard screenshots, training materials, executive presentations)

ABOUT YOU

This role requires a minimum of three days per week working together in person, either at a client location or at your Bain home office. Travel is required. This position requires regional travel away from your home office/primary working location. Travel frequency and destinations will vary based on project needs. 

  • BA or equivalent
  • Distinct commercial excellence experience, ideally experience running complex analytics and working with large datasets, as well as business consulting experience
  • Proficiency in data science and analytics tools (e.g., Python, SQL, R), visualization platforms (e.g., Tableau), and experience building RAG-based and agentic AI systems
  • We are looking for candidates that fit at least one of the below criteria:
    • 1+ year/s of experience as a Bain consultant  or Bain AAG Client Lead
    • 4+ years of industry level experience in a data science or commercial operations role (MBA preferred in this case)
  • Ability to quickly grasp and apply complex concepts
  • Strong abilities in problem definition and problem-solving
  • Excellent analytical capabilities and experience using complex modeling and analysis to inform key business decisions
  • Excellent oral, written, and PowerPoint communication skills
  • Proven ability to work effectively, and manage others in diverse and distributed teams
  • Ability to work well with all levels within Bain and at client
  • Ability to lead interactions with senior stakeholders (at Bain or Client)
  • Comfort working independently and/or remotely from supervisors in a fast-paced environment
  • Willingness to travel to client locations (expected 20-40%, potential peak of 60%)

Preferred:

  • MBA, or PhD in a technical field. Advanced understanding of data science methodologies, including machine learning and modeling, with a strong analytical background
  • Formal Agile/SCRUM certification. Experience supporting deployment of SaaS solutions with customers/clients or similar roles. Experience in software customer success roles. Experience in technology product management and project management.

 

U.S. Compensation Information 

Compensation for this role includes base salary, annual discretionary performance bonus, 401(k) plan with an annual employer contribution based on years of service and Bain’s best in class benefits package (details listed below).

Some local governments in the United States require a good-faith, reasonable salary range to be included in job postings for open roles. The estimated annualized compensation for this role is as follows:

  • In New York, California, Texas, Massachusetts, Illinois, and Georgia, the good-faith, reasonable annualized full-time salary range for this role is between $128,500-$171,500; placement within this range will vary based on several factors including, but not limited to experience, education, licensure/certifications, training and skill level  
  • For all other locations, the good-faith, reasonable annualized full-time salary range for this role is commensurate with competitive geographic market rates for this role and will vary based on several factors including, but not limited to experience, education, licensure/certifications, training and skill level  
  • Annual discretionary performance bonus  
    • This role may also be eligible for other elements of discretionary compensation  
  • 4.5% 401(k) company contribution, which increases after 3 years of service and is 100% vested upon start date 

Bain & Company's comprehensive benefits and wellness program is designed to help employees achieve personal independence, protection and stability in the areas most important to you and your family. 

Bain pays 100% individual employee premiums for medical, dental and vision programs, offering one of the most comprehensive medical plans for employees without impacting your paycheck  

  • Generous paid time off, including parental leave, sick leave and paid holidays 
  • Fully vested 401(k) company contribution 
  • Paid Life and Long-Term Disability insurance 
  • Annual fitness reimbursements 

It is unlawful in Massachusetts to require or administer a lie detector test as a condition of employment or continued employment. An employer who violates this law shall be subject to criminal penalties and civil liability.
job.title: Expert Consultant, Data Sciences, Coro

job.company_description:
Bain & Company is a global consultancy that helps the world’s most ambitious change makers define the future.
Across 65 cities in 40 countries, we work alongside our clients as one team with a shared ambition to achieve extraordinary results, outperform the competition, and redefine industries. We complement our tailored, integrated expertise with a vibrant ecosystem of digital innovators to deliver better, faster, and more enduring outcomes. Our 10-year commitment to invest more than $1 billion in pro bono services brings our talent, expertise, and insight to organizations tackling today’s urgent challenges in education, racial equity, social justice, economic development, and the environment. We earned a platinum rating from EcoVadis, the leading platform for environmental, social, and ethical performance ratings for global supply chains, putting us in the top 1% of all companies. Since our founding in 1973, we have measured our success by the success of our clients, and we proudly maintain the highest level of client
job.job_description:
WHAT MAKES US A GREAT PLACE TO WORK


We are proud to be consistently recognized as one of the world’s best places to work. We are currently the top ranked consulting firm on Glassdoor’s Best Places to Work  list and have earned the #1 overall spot a record seven times. Extraordinary teams are at the heart of our business strategy, but these don’t happen by chance. They require intentional focus on bringing together a broad set of backgrounds, cultures, experiences, perspectives, and skills in a supportive and inclusive work environment. We hire people with exceptional talent and create an environment in which every individual can thrive professionally and personally.   


WHO YOU’LL WORK WITH
You’ll join Bain’s Tech Solutions BBS team, a trusted advisor to the business on technology. Our team partners across functions to understand core business processes, provide consultation to solve challenges, and ensure business value is defined, realized, and recognized. We focus on cross-functional challenges and end-to-end processes, delivering a world-class, customer-centric technology experience that underpins Bain’s strategy to out-team, out-innovate, and out-grow as we play for #1.

 

 

WHERE YOU’LL FIT WITHIN THE TEAM
As a Senior Analyst, TSG Product Management, you’ll play a critical role in Bain’s global implementation of Salesforce.com. You’ll translate customer needs into an actionable backlog, then partner with Global Commercial Operations, stakeholders, and technical teams to ensure successful product delivery.

 

 

WHAT YOU’LL DO

Drive change requests and stakeholder engagement (50%)

  • Understand key business objectives and product strategy from leadership
  • Elicit requirements for enhancements, new functionality, and integrations
  • Identify, track, and analyze key metrics to drive actionable insights
  • Support stakeholder communication, pilots, and training
  • Troubleshoot issues, identify root causes, and translate into user stories

Product delivery (40%)

  • Participate in Agile ceremonies for this product
  • Transform requirements into user stories with technical detail and supporting materials
  • Collaborate with technical teams and vendors to optimize Bain’s experience
  • Test, validate, and provide input on work in progress
  • Manage user provisioning and deprovisioning, ensuring accuracy and compliance
  • Support release management activities and communications

Domain knowledge (10%)

  • Build and maintain expertise in Bain’s internal processes and external market trends

 

 

ABOUT YOU

  • Bachelor’s degree in Business, Information Systems, Computer Science, or equivalent experience
  • 2+ years of experience as a product owner, business analyst, or similar role
  • Proficient with Agile methodologies
  • Strong communication and interpersonal skills with a customer-first mindset
  • Skilled in defining, documenting, and breaking down requirements
  • Collaborative thought partner who works well across teams
  • Experience with Salesforce.com Sales Cloud required; certification preferred (Admin, Business Analyst)
  • Willingness to travel if needed

 


 

U.S. COMPENSATION INFORMATION

Compensation for this role includes base salary, annual discretionary performance bonus, 401(k) plan with an annual employer contribution based on years of service, and Bain’s best-in-class benefits package.

 

Some local governments in the United States require a good-faith, reasonable salary range be included in job postings for open roles. The estimated annualized compensation for this role is as follows:

 

  • In Georgia state, the good-faith, reasonable annualized full-time salary range for this role is between $92,500 - $110,500; placement within this range will vary based on several factors including, but not limited to, experience, education, licensure/certifications, training, and skill level.

 

  • In Illinois, the good-faith, reasonable annualized full-time salary range for this role is between $101,000 - $121,500; placement within this range will vary based on several factors including, but not limited to, experience, education, licensure/certifications, training, and skill level.

 

  • In Massachusetts, the good-faith, reasonable annualized full-time salary range for this role is between $106,000 - $127,000; placement within this range will vary based on several factors including, but not limited to, experience, education, licensure/certifications, training, and skill level.

 

  • In Texas, the good-faith, reasonable annualized full-time salary range for this role is between $96,500 - $116,000; placement within this range will vary based on several factors including, but not limited to, experience, education, licensure/certifications, training, and skill level.

 

Additional compensation may include:

  • Annual discretionary performance bonus
  • 4.5% 401(k) company contribution, increasing after 3 years and fully vested upon start
  • Full coverage of individual medical, dental, and vision premiums
  • Generous paid time off, including parental leave and holidays
  • Paid life and long-term disability insurance
  • Annual fitness reimbursement

 

MASSACHUSETTS EMPLOYMENT NOTICE

It is unlawful in Massachusetts to require or administer a lie detector test as a condition of employment or continued employment. An employer who violates this law shall be subject to criminal penalties and civil liability.
job.title: Senior Analyst, Technical Product Management - Salesforce/SalesCloud

job.company_description:
BCD Travel creates connections that move people and ideas forward. Through open technology and trusted human expertise, we help companies and people navigate change, simplify complexity and make confident decisions about how and when they travel. Our intuitive digital experiences for every stakeholder power journeys that fuel success and drive progress.
With 15,000+ dedicated team members serving clients in 170+ countries, BCD is shaping a more sustainable future for business travel. Industry-leading meetings and events management and a global consultancy complete our suite of solutions and services. In 2024, BCD achieved US$22.9 billion in sales.
For more information, visit our website. You can also subscribe to our newsletter for the latest industry insights, thought-leader analysis and top tips to drive your successful travel program. Go to: http://ow.ly/N73i50Mhc9Y
job.job_description:
Start your journey with BCD: Grow, connect, collaborate and celebrate with our global team

Senior Director, Travel & Expense (T&E) Management Integration (Remote)

Full time, Remote United States, UK, Spain, Poland, Netherlands, Germany, Denmark, France, Belgium

The Senior Director of T&E Management Integration is a strategic business leader responsible for shaping and executing the vision for seamless integration between travel booking, payment, and expense management ecosystems. This role focuses on driving partnerships and interoperability with leading expense management platforms to deliver measurable value for clients through improved compliance, cost control, and traveler experience.

You will lead cross-functional initiatives that align product strategy, commercial objectives, and partner engagement to create differentiated solutions that strengthen our competitive position in the global travel and payments market.

As a Senior Director, Travel & Expense (T&E) Management Integration, you will

  • Define and own the global strategy for T&E integration, ensuring alignment with corporate objectives and client needs
  • Develop competitive differentiation through advanced integration capabilities that reduce friction and optimize spend visibility
  • Partner with Finance and Commercial teams to create business cases and ROI models demonstrating value for clients and internal stakeholders
  • Build and maintain executive-level relationships with major expense management providers
  • Establish tiered integration frameworks (basic, advanced, premium) to support diverse client requirements and maturity levels
  • Drive adoption of standardized APIs, connectors, and accelerators to reduce implementation complexity and time-to-value
  • Collaborate with Sales Enablement and Marketing to create integration-focused value propositions, RFP libraries, and executive presentation materials
  • Support strategic client engagements, workshops, and QBRs to position integration as a business enabler rather than a technical feature

About You

  • Extensive experience in strategic product leadership or business integration roles within Expense Management, FinTech, or Enterprise SaaS
  • Proven success in building partnerships with expense management platforms and delivering integrated solutions at scale
  • Strong understanding of expense workflows, compliance frameworks, and financial governance
  • Executive presence with ability to influence C-level stakeholders internally and externally
  • MBA or equivalent experience preferred

About Us

We’re a market leader in travel management: We help people and companies travel smart and achieve more, and our clients include some of the world’s best-known and most innovative business and consumer brands. We operate in more than 100 countries, and the majority of our 10,000+ people work virtually (because a 10-second commute usually makes awesome sense). We’re committed to sustainability and to driving success for our customers, our business and ourselves. Learn more about BCD Travel.

Your life at BCD

Working at BCD means connecting, collaborating and celebrating. You’ll be part of a virtual, dynamic and flexible work environment that fosters a good work-life balance so you can focus on what makes you happy. But don’t just take our word for it! Hear why people join BCD and more about the great perks of Life at BCD.

You’ll be offered

  • Flexible working hours and work-from-home or remote opportunities
  • Opportunities to grow your skillset and career
  • Generous vacation days so you can rest and recharge
  • A compensation package that feels fair to you, including mental, physical, and financial wellbeing tools
  • Travel industry professional perks and discounts
  • An inclusive work environment where diversity is celebrated

At BCD Travel we value you, our culture and success is defined by you. We carefully consider a wide range of compensation factors, including your professional background and experience. The salary range for candidates in applicable jurisdictions in the US for the position of Senior Director, Travel & Expense (T&E) Management Integration is between $130,000 and $150,000. The actual pay depends on your skills, qualifications, experience and geographical location.

Ready to join the journey? Apply now!

We’re dedicated to building a diverse, inclusive and authentic workplace. If you’re excited about a role, but your experience doesn’t align perfectly, we still encourage you to apply.

We are committed to providing reasonable and necessary accommodations to ensure all employees can perform their roles effectively. For accommodation requests or further information, contact our Talent Acquisition department at careers@bcdtravel.com.

Start your journey with BCD: Grow, connect, collaborate and celebrate with our global team

Regional Program Manager

Full time, United States

As a Regional Program Manager, you are responsible for managing a single client account or portfolio of a regional account(s) to include but not limited to client retention, contract negotiation, implementation, business consolidation, sales of products and services, financials, business planning, and reviews, establishing relationships with key decision makers in multiple regions, and staff development. You’ll report to global program management.

As a Regional Program Manager, you will

  • Oversee the regional coordination of all communication across the region, both BCD Travel and the client’s global initiatives
  • Manage new business implementations, working closely with the implementation team to ensure client expectations are met
  • Help measure and report Service Level Agreements (SLAs) and Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) on a regional level and develop deliberate business plans
  • Drive profitability of the contract on a regional basis by increasing revenues through up-selling and cross-selling to meet client’s strategic needs
  • Identify and assist in the selection of technology tools and processes to produce desired results
  • Establish and maintain relationships with key customer decision-makers in the region
  • Perform client business reviews and deliver regular financial reporting to all stakeholders

About You

  • Prior travel management company or related business travel account management experience desired
  • Experience implementing and managing large market or regional clients
  • Thorough knowledge of business travel account management and operations
  • Adept with travel industry technology and processes
  • Demonstrated ability to successfully build, maintain, and grow customer portfolios and client relationships
  • Knowledge of creating business plans and managing business/client strategies
  • Previous experience in managing cross-functional work teams
  • Effective presentation skills in terms of development and delivery
  • Highly proficient in spoken and written English

  • Flexible working hours and work-from-home or remote opportunities
  • Opportunities to grow your skillset and career
  • Generous vacation days so you can rest and recharge
  • A compensation package that feels fair to you, including mental, physical, and financial wellbeing tools

At BCD Travel we value you, our culture and success is defined by you. We carefully consider a wide range of compensation factors, including your professional background and experience. The salary range for candidates in applicable jurisdictions in the US for the position of Regional Account Manager is between $75,000 and $80,000. The actual pay depends on your skills, qualifications, experience and geographical location.

Ready to join the journey? Apply now!

We’re dedicated to building a diverse, inclusive and authentic workplace. If you’re excited about a role, but your experience doesn’t align perfectly, we still encourage you to apply.

job.job_description:
Start your journey with BCD: Grow, connect, collaborate and celebrate with our global team

Senior Travel Consultant (Remote)

Full time, United States

In this role, you will perform high-touch service excellence to key client travelers and be responsible for accurately and efficiently handling incoming requests via multiple channels (i.e. phone, email, etc.).

You will provide active travel consultation, including providing travel details and up-selling related products.

As a Senior Travel Consultant, you will

  • Search and confirm travel reservations for the customer (being Consultative in offer better routings, fares, round the world fares, alternative hotel options)
  • Have a strong understanding of a client travel policy and can appropriately guide and consistently provide consultation to the customer
  • Fulfill all requests regarding necessary regulations (DOT, TSA, passports, visas, etc.)
  • Excellent knowledge and application of travel supplier rules
  • Maintain, actively communicate, and seek expansion of current knowledge of the state of the various travel industries supported
  • Expertly use the appropriate the BCD Travel tools and systems to complete requests
  • My ARC
  • I-Sight (logging Complaints, Compliments, and Value Adds)
  • Airline Portals (AA Sales link, Delta Pro, United JetStream)
  • GroundSpan
  • OKB
  • IRD/DMED
  • All aspects of GPoS (including setting up exchanges)
  • Voice and Email on AgentSource Compass
  • Queue work

About You

  • Strong skills in international reservations
  • Working knowledge of the travel industry, policies, procedures, and processes
  • Strong skills in GDS Sabre and BCD applications
  • Strong verbal and written communication skills in English

We’re a market leader in travel management: We help people and companies travel smart and achieve more, and our clients include some of the world’s best-known and most innovative business and consumer brands.   • Flexible working hours and work-from-home or remote opportunities
  • Opportunities to grow your skillset and career
  • Generous vacation days so you can rest and recharge
  • A compensation package that feels fair to you, including mental, physical, and financial wellbeing tools
  • Travel industry professional perks and discounts
  • An inclusive work environment where diversity is celebrated

At BCD Travel we value you, our culture and success is defined by you. We carefully consider a wide range of compensation factors, including your professional background and experience. The salary range for candidates in applicable jurisdictions in the US for the position of Senior Travel Consultant is between $48,000 and $60,000. The actual pay depends on your skills, qualifications, experience and geographical location.

We reserve the right to change schedules to accommodate operational needs. Flexibility in scheduling is a core requirement of the role.

job.company_description: Choice Hotels International, Inc. (NYSE: CHH) is one of the largest lodging franchisors in the world. The challenger in upscale and a leader in midscale and extended stay, Choice® has nearly 7,500 hotels, representing more than 630,000 rooms, in 45 countries and territories. A diverse portfolio of 22 brands that range from full-service upper upscale properties to midscale, extended stay, and economy enables Choice® to meet travelers' needs in more places and for more occasions while driving more value for franchise owners and shareholders. The award-winning Choice Privileges® rewards program and co-brand credit card options provide members with a fast and easy way to earn reward nights and personalized perks. For more information, visit www.choicehotels.com. At our worldwide corporate headquarters in the Washington metropolitan area (North Bethesda, MD), our IT center in Scottsdale, AZ, and Minneapolis, MN, and through our associates across the globe, we keep those hotels humming by serving as a champion for our franchisees. When you #MakeItYourChoice, you will have the environment, the tools, and the momentum to drive your career and organizational success.
job.job_description:
JOB SUMMARY:

The Senior Manager, Customer Relationship Management is a strategic marketing role focused on driving growth and loyalty through personalized email and SMS communications for Choice Hotels’ loyalty members and brand partners. This role exists to strengthen Choice Privileges member engagement, increase lifetime value, and generate incremental revenue by delivering relevant, timely messaging across the customer journey. Reporting to the Director of Loyalty Marketing Communications, the Senior Manager leads CRM strategy and execution, including customer journey design and cross-channel marketing orchestration. The role partners closely with internal teams, external partners, and agencies to ensure alignment with broader marketing and business objectives and plays a key leadership role in guiding cross-functional stakeholders.

RESPONSIBILITIES:

Email Strategy and Execution

  • Drive revenue through email marketing: Lead the strategy, execution, and optimization of member and partnership email campaigns that increase purchases and brand loyalty, supported by a robust test-and-learn roadmap.
  • Craft data-driven strategies: Design personalized customer journeys to support key segments and partners across multiple touchpoints, maximizing engagement and ROI.
  • Cultivate strong partnerships: Partner with internal teams and external stakeholders to ensure campaign alignment, performance transparency, and shared learning.
  • Manage a talented team: Guide and mentor CRM Analyst(s), fostering their growth and contribution.

Loyalty and Partnership Orchestration

  • New revenue opportunities: Collaborate with key stakeholders to identify and develop high-impact marketing campaigns that align with Choice Hotels and partner needs.
  • Lead cross-channel alignment: Develop and manage the annual email campaign calendar, prioritizing initiatives based on organizational goals and maximizing loyalty marketing success.
  • Drive campaign execution: Partner with internal teams to secure all necessary content and assets, ensuring seamless and timely deployment of marketing campaigns.
  • Enable integrated marketing: Collaborate with Brand Strategy and Partnership teams to create comprehensive marketing strategies, amplifying external marketing efforts for Choice Hotels and our partners.

Marketing SME

  • Be an industry trailblazer: Monitor industry trends, MarTech advancements, and competitor efforts to identify opportunities that enhance Choice Hotels’ omni-channel marketing approach.
  • Champion the consumer voice: Articulate customer perspectives and marketing strategies to internal and external audiences, representing Choice Hotels' marketing efforts with confidence and clarity.
  • Drive marketing innovation: Champion a culture of data-driven testing and learning, partnering with Content, Creative, and Digital teams to continuously improve performance.
  • Quantify marketing impact: Translate campaign performance into actionable insights and senior management-ready reporting that demonstrates ROI.

QUALIFICATIONS:

Employment Experience

  • 6-8 years extensive experience in Customer Relationship Management
  • Deep expertise in the email channel and insights to drive strategy across the customer journey, including previous hands-on execution of email, SMS, and lifecycle touchpoints.
  • Highly analytical, with the ability to translate insights into action and inform a test-and-learn roadmap that improves loyalty and channel effectiveness.
  • Strong understanding of integrated marketing communications across traditional and digital channels, with experience managing marketing initiatives and evaluating campaign performance metrics.
  • Collaborative and influential leader with the ability to drive alignment across internal teams and external partners.
  • Must be decisive, with strong strategic thinking.

Technical Skills

  • Strong verbal, written and listening communication skills.
  • Strong interpersonal skills and demeanor.
  • Excellent project management skills.
  • Excellent analytical and problem-solving skills.
  • Strong attention to detail and accuracy.
  • Strong presentation skills.
  • Proficient in the use of MS Office applications such as Outlook, Word, PowerPoint and Excel.
  • Basic understanding and skill in building emails.

Additional Skills & Competencies

  • Ability to work in a team-oriented and cross-functional environment.
  • Ability to work independently and collaboratively.
  • Ability to develop and manage successful internal and external relationships.
  • Ability to uphold Choice’s Values & Performance Principles of collaboration, performance excellence, sense of urgency, openness to new ideas, inclusion & diversity, integrity, customer focus, and respect.

Education Requirements

  • Bachelor's degree in Marketing, Advertising or Communications, or related field, from a four-year college or university; or equivalent combination of education and experience, is preferred.

Salary Range

The salary range for this position is $123,663 - $142,600 annually. In addition to the annual salary, this role is eligible for an annual bonus based on the terms of Choice's Management Incentive Plan (MIP).

Choice prioritizes our associate wellbeing by offering a comprehensive benefits program that is both competitive and flexible to help you achieve your wellbeing goals - here are just a few:

  • Competitive compensation and benefits, including medical, dental, and vision coverage
  • Leave and paid time-off for holidays, vacation, personal, family, volunteer, sick, jury duty, bereavement, military, and religious observance
  • Financial benefits for retirement and health savings
  • Employee recognition programs
  • Discounts at Choice hotels worldwide

About Choice

Choice Hotels International, Inc. (NYSE: CHH), is one of the largest lodging franchisors in the world. With 7,500 hotels in 45+ countries and territories, we offer a range of high-quality lodging options in the upper upscale, upper midscale, midscale, extended stay, and economy segments. We’re the hotel company for those who choose to bet on themselves — the striver, the dreamer, the entrepreneur — because that’s who we are, too.

At Choice, we are united by the simple belief that tomorrow will be even better than today — for associates, our company, and our franchisees. At our worldwide corporate headquarters in North Bethesda, Maryland, at our technology center in Scottsdale, Arizona, and through our associates around the globe, every voice is heard and every idea is listened to, no matter what area of the company they come from. We are united in supporting the entrepreneurial dreams of our thousands of franchise owners, which propels us forward — giving our work at Choice a purpose larger than our business.

Our corporate office locations:

North Bethesda, MD — Located at Pike & Rose, our worldwide headquarters is less than 15 miles from Washington, D.C., one block away from the North Bethesda Metro station, with easy access to I-495, complimentary parking, electronic charging stations, restaurants and retail.

Scottsdale, AZ — Located at the northwest corner of Loop 101, the Scottsdale office is home to our technology, eCommerce and customer service organizations, with easy access to complimentary parking, electronic charging stations, restaurants and retail.

Minneapolis, MN — Select roles are based in our Minneapolis office on Highway 394, near the intersection with Highway 100, only five minutes from downtown.

Field/Remote — Select roles designated as field/remote will require associates to work from a home office, connecting virtually with Choice team members and leadership on Zoom, with possible required travel depending on the role.

Choice’s Cultural Values

Welcome and Respect Everyone | Be Bold | Be Quick | Listen | Be Curious | Show Integrity

Choice’s Leadership Principles

Act with Intention | Lead with Authenticity | Grow & Deliver
