job.job_description:
JOB SUMMARY:

The Corporate FP&A Manager supports the orchestration and consolidation of the enterprise-level annual budget and forecast and prepares high-quality analysis for senior leaders, including materials for review by the VP of FP&A, CFO, CEO and the Board of Directors. This role impacts business performance through rigorous financial modeling and analytics, standardized processes, and strong collaboration to create financial plans that align with strategic and operational plans. This role is highly visible and hands-on, balancing strategic analysis with detailed execution and ownership of deliverables.

RESPONSIBILITIES:

Enterprise-Level Consolidated Financial Planning

  • Prepares financial analysis leveraged during the execution of the annual enterprise-level budget and quarterly/monthly forecasts
  • Prepares forecast/close instructions, process / system information, upcoming deadlines, and clear guidelines and parameters to ensure processes are met in a timely manner and adhere to quality standards.
  • Support the preparation and execution of the annual risk mitigation plan that ensure annual targets are met across the company
  • Prepares materials to create financial grids used for the management incentive plan and consolidates the performance vs. actuals for those metrics
  • Prepares consolidated materials for finance functions such as tax or accounting (forecast used for tax provisions, accrual for bonuses, etc.)

Enterprise Level Financial Analysis

  • Prepares materials for the monthly pre-close and close variance analysis, identifying key drivers of change
  • Delivers monthly enterprise-level financial analysis on performance vs. actuals, identify trends and financial modeling/analytics to advise VP of FP&A on areas of risk opportunities
  • Identifies variances between plan of record and actuals and reaches out to relevant financial or operational stakeholders to articulate the story behind the numbers at the enterprise level, while mastering enough of the underlying functional data to explain high level drivers of functional businesses.
  • Support the preparation of materials for Board of Directors, Executive Meetings and strategic discussions.
  • Supports the roadmap for financial systems enhancement, helping to gather requests from the rest of FP&A and consolidating for prioritization.
  • Identifies AI use cases for FP&A consolidation and variance analysis.
  • Prepares enterprise level presentations used in executive offsites / leadership meetings
  • Prepares analysis and reporting leveraged by the Investor Relations, including the formulation of data-driven, informed, concise answers to the Q&A on a quarterly basis, leveraging understanding of the business and tapping into the relevant FP&A functions when appropriate.

Special projects financial analysis

Provide financial analysis for the following type of projects:

  • FP&A support for M&A, including synergies coordination, integration into the financials etc.
  • Enterprise-wide workforce planning projects
  • Large corporate finance changes for the company
  • Ancillary opportunities, Business divestitures and reorganizations, Confidential projects

QUALIFICATIONS:

Employment Experience

  • 4+ years with financial analysis and financial or strategic planning experience, preferably with hospitality industry experience

Technical Skills

  • Proficient in Microsoft Office, with advanced knowledge of Excel (financial modeling techniques) -Ability to manage large amounts of data and present findings in a clear and concise way
  • Experience using and managing CPM tools; experience with Oracle Fusion/Smart View a plus
  • General Knowledge of FASB and SEC requirements, Generally Accepted Accounting Principles, tax reporting and accounting practices
  • Ability to communicate and explain complex financial issues to non-financial professionals and to interact well with all levels within the organization
  • High degree of analytical capability, with proven experience developing best-in-class financial models and leveraging analysis to influence decision-making

Additional Skills & Competencies

  • Ability to take on small to medium projects and own their delivery
  • Ability to assess a situation and make decisions even when time is critical and data incomplete
  • Excellent business-savvy with a consultative approach; ability to collaborate with senior leaders across organizational lines
  • Self-motivated to work independently and collaboratively in a team-oriented environment
  • Results-oriented with a strong sense of ownership

Education Requirements

  • Bachelor’s degree in Finance, Accounting, Economics, or related field, or equivalent combination of education and work experience.

WORK LOCATION:

This position is based at Choice Hotels’ Pike & Rose headquarters in North Bethesda, Maryland. The selected candidate must be able to commit to this location requirement and adhere to the company’s hybrid work schedule, which consists of four days onsite and one day remote per week.

NOTE:

This position is not eligible for relocation assistance or sponsorship.

Salary Range

The salary range for this position is $100,000 to $115,000 annually. In addition to the annual salary, this role is eligible for an annual bonus based on the terms of Choice's Management Incentive Plan (MIP).

The pay range listed is for this position and is what Choice Hotels reasonably expects to pay. We may ultimately pay more or less than the posted range, and the range may be modified in the future. An employee’s pay position within the pay range will be based on several factors including, but not limited to, relevant education, qualifications, qualifications, qualifications, certifications, experience, skills, seniority, geographic location, performance, shift, travel requirements, sales or revenue-based metrics, and business or organizational needs.

Choice prioritizes our associate wellbeing by offering a comprehensive benefits program that is both competitive and flexible to help you achieve your wellbeing goals - here are just a few:

  • Competitive compensation and benefits, including medical, dental, and vision coverage
  • Leave and paid time-off for holidays, vacation, personal, family, volunteer, sick, jury duty, bereavement, military, and religious observance
  • Financial benefits for retirement and health savings
  • Employee recognition programs
  • Discounts at Choice hotels worldwide

About Choice

Choice Hotels International, Inc. (NYSE: CHH), is one of the largest lodging franchisors in the world. With 7,500 hotels in 45+ countries and territories, we offer a range of high-quality lodging options in the upper upscale, upper midscale, midscale, extended stay, and economy segments. We’re the hotel company for those who choose to bet on themselves — the striver, the dreamer, the entrepreneur — because that’s who we are, too.

At Choice, we are united by the simple belief that tomorrow will be even better than today — for associates, our company, and our franchisees. At our worldwide corporate headquarters in North Bethesda, Maryland, at our technology center in Scottsdale, Arizona, and through our associates around the globe, every voice is heard and every idea is listened to, no matter what area of the company they come from. We are united in supporting the entrepreneurial dreams of our thousands of franchise owners, which propels us forward — giving our work at Choice a purpose larger than our business.

Our corporate office locations:

North Bethesda, MD — Located at Pike & Rose, our worldwide headquarters is less than 15 miles from Washington, D.C., one block away from the North Bethesda Metro station, with easy access to I-495, complimentary parking, electronic charging stations, restaurants and retail.

Scottsdale, AZ — Located at the northwest corner of Loop 101, the Scottsdale office is home to our technology, eCommerce and customer service organizations, with easy access to complimentary parking, electronic charging stations, restaurants and retail.

Minneapolis, MN — Select roles are based in our Minneapolis office on Highway 394, near the intersection with Highway 100, only five minutes from downtown.

Field/Remote — Select roles designated as field/remote will require associates to work from a home office, connecting virtually with Choice team members and leadership on Zoom, with possible required travel depending on the role.

Choice’s Cultural Values

Welcome and Respect Everyone | Be Bold | Be Quick | Listen | Be Curious | Show Integrity

Choice’s Leadership Principles

Act with Intention | Lead with Authenticity | Grow & Deliver
job.title: Corporate FP&A Manager

job.company_description:
Delta Air Lines (NYSE: DAL) is the U.S. global airline leader in safety, innovation, reliability and customer experience. Powered by our employees around the world, Delta has for a decade led the airline industry in operational excellence while maintaining our reputation for award-winning customer service.
With our mission of connecting the people and cultures of the globe, Delta strives to foster understanding across a diverse world and serve as a force for social good.
job.job_description:
How You'll Help Us Keep Climbing (Overview & Key Responsibilities)

Become a member of Delta’s World Class DOM PRM team as a Domestic Revenue Management Specialist. Delta’s RM professionals are responsible for maximizing the revenue generated on each flight and customer segment by aligning passenger demand with willingness to pay to offer the optimal mix of fare products. Core to Delta’s industry-leading revenue results, each Specialist uses a rigorous, innovative set of demand management and competitive pricing strategies. Every Revenue Management Specialist blends the art of demand management with the science of analytical expertise to create the right price for each customer on every flight. 

As part of the department onboarding process, you will participate in a training cohort with the expectation of successful completion of the training courses and knowledge

DOM PRM Team Functions

  • If placed in a Pricing role, you will monitor, initiate and communicate industry pricing actions including changes to fare levels, fare rules, policies, and pricing strategy execution
    • Proactively manage Delta’s fare structure including prices and fare rules
    • Identify and analyze market issues and trends, determining appropriate pricing actions, filing of actions, and post-audit assessment of actions
  • If placed in an Inventory management role, you will be responsible for flight level revenue optimization by properly valuing our passengers and forecasting future demand levels
    • Monitor and update the flight level demand forecasts through the analysis of historical and future booking trends, the pricing environment, industry capacity trends, the competitive landscape, and other factors
    • Establish and maintain the flight level overbooking forecasts through the analysis of no-show behavior trends
  • Own and manage high revenue impact markets with complex dynamics, competitive pressures, and strategic importance.
  • Use various and complex high-level dashboards, prescriptive to descriptive analysis, along with market specific data, to create an appropriate customer offer to best segment customers and maximize revenue
  • Diagnose, interpret, and communicate basic market performance of revenue stats: LF, Yield, RASM, local vs. flow, drivers and actions
  • Evaluate performance and post-departure trends to identify and leverage future opportunities while maximizing the revenue potential for each market
  • Performing frequent ad-hoc analyses related to the assigned markets
  • Communicate market trends, updates, recommendations and overall performance results to Revenue Management leaders and key stakeholders within the Commercial team
  • Mentor Analysts and Senior Analysts, providing guidance on market strategy, tools, and best practices.
  • Provide technical support to other revenue management groups and both internal and external distribution channels and reporting
  • Work cross-functionally with Inventory Management, Pricing, Network Planning, Alliances, Sales, Marketing, and other teams to ensure alignment on market level strategies
What You Need To Succeed (Minimum Qualifications)

  • 5+ years of relevant experience in Revenue Management, Pricing, Inventory, or related analytical roles.
  • Detail-oriented, analytical ability are required
  • Strong presentation, public speaking and written communication skills are required
  • Demonstrate ability to manage the frequent market dynamic changes quickly and function in a high pace environment
  • Must be able to set priorities in a time sensitive environment based on revenue impact and be able to manage several responsibilities at once
  • Must be intellectually curious, interested in problem solving where multiple variables are impacting market performance
  • Must be able to work efficiently as part of a team and as an individual contributor, embracing diverse people, thinking and styles
  • Good working knowledge of Microsoft Office software is required
  • Must be performing satisfactorily in current position
  • Consistently prioritizes safety and security of self, others, and personal data
  • Embraces diverse people, thinking, and styles
  • Possesses a high school diploma, GED, or high school equivalency
  • Is at least 18 years of age and has authorization to work in the United States

What Will Give You a Competitive Edge (Preferred Qualifications)

  • Demonstrate ability to manage the frequent market dynamic changes quickly and function in a high pace environment

Benefits And Perks To Help You Keep Climbing

Our culture is rooted in a shared dedication to living our values – Care, Integrity, Resilience, Servant Leadership, and Teamwork – every day, in everything we do. At Delta, our people are our success. At the heart of what we offer is our focus on Sharing Success with Delta employees. Exploring a career at Delta gives you a chance to see the world while earning great compensation and benefits to help you keep climbing along the way:

  • Competitive salary, industry-leading proﬁt sharing program, and performance incentives
  • 401(k) with generous company contributions up to 9%
  • Paid time off including vacation, holidays, paid personal time, maternity and parental leave
  • Comprehensive health beneﬁts including medical, dental, vision, short/long term disability and life beneﬁts
  • Family care assistance through fertility support, surrogacy and adoption assistance, lactation support, subsidized back-up care, and programs that help with loved ones in all stages
  • Holistic Wellbeing programs to support physical, emotional, social, and financial health, including access to an employee assistance program offering support for you and anyone in your household, free financial coaching, and extensive resources supporting mental health
  • Domestic and International space-available flight privileges for employees and eligible family members
  • Career development programs to achieve your long-term career goals
  • World-wide partnerships to engage in community service and innovative goals created to focus on sustainability and reducing our carbon footprint
  • Business Resource Groups created to connect employees with common interests to promote inclusion, provide perspective and help implement strategies
  • Recognition rewards and awards through the platform Unstoppable Together
  • Access to over 500 discounts, specialty savings and voluntary benefits through Deltaperks such as car and hotel rentals and auto, home, and pet insurance, legal services, and childcare
job.title: Specialist, Domestic Revenue Management

job.company_description:
Delta Air Lines (NYSE: DAL) is the U.S. global airline leader in safety, innovation, reliability and customer experience. Powered by our employees around the world, Delta has for a decade led the airline industry in operational excellence while maintaining our reputation for award-winning customer service.
With our mission of connecting the people and cultures of the globe, Delta strives to foster understanding across a diverse world and serve as a force for social good.
job.job_description:
How You'll Help Us Keep Climbing (Overview & Key Responsibilities)

At Delta Air Lines, connection is at the heart of everything we do and guides our every action. We strive to welcome and care for all of our customers during their travels with us and aim to deliver an elevated experience.

We are seeking a Grade 7 Customer Trip Product Owner to own the context building of trip details and steward the enterprise trip data repository. You will define how trip events, customer and flight attributes, and operational signals are captured at the lowest data level to create a trustworthy, real-time source of truth. This role also enables internal customers to onboard to the platform and partners closely with the Notifications Business Rules Engine to feed timely, accurate payloads for customer communications.

Scope & Impact

  • Owns the trip context domain and the enterprise trip data repository—schemas, events, relationships, and governance.
  • Influences Customer and Flight data product roadmaps to ensure essential context is modeled and maintained at the lowest data level.
  • Enables internal customers (operations, care, digital, analytics) to onboard and build on the platform with reliable APIs and documentation.
  • Feeds the Notifications Business Rules Engine with timely and accurate context to power correct template selection and payload construction.

Tools & Platforms

  • Data: Event streaming, ETL/ELT pipelines, data catalog/lineage, schema registries, monitoring/alerting.
  • Identity & Consent: Customer ID resolution, preference management, consent capture and enforcement.
  • Notifications: Business Rules Engine, template services, push gateways, SMS aggregators, email providers.
  • Delivery & Docs: Backlog/roadmap tools, API gateways, developer portals, documentation platforms.

Key Responsibilities

  • Strategy & Roadmap
  • Define a multi-quarter roadmap for trip context, repository capabilities, and platform enablement tied to measurable outcomes.
  • Translate objectives into epics/features with clear acceptance criteria and dependencies across Customer and Flight data products.
  • Prioritize based on customer impact, operational risk, compliance, and technical feasibility; manage trade-offs transparently.
  • Trip Context Data Management
  • Own canonical trip schemas, event definitions, and relationships across bookings, changes, disruptions, and resolutions.
  • Set data quality SLAs and validation rules (freshness, completeness, accuracy, consistency); drive remediation for defects.
  • Ensure context is modeled at the lowest level necessary to support eligibility, personalization, and compliance use cases.
  • Partner with engineering on event-driven architectures and APIs; define contracts and versioning for backward compatibility.
  • Platform Onboarding & Enablement
  • Create onboarding guides, sample payloads, SDKs, and reference integrations for internal customers.
  • Run intake, discovery, and readiness assessments; align on use cases, data needs, guardrails, and service levels.
  • Establish support processes (runbooks, SLAs, office hours) and feedback loops to improve developer experience.
  • Integration with Notifications Business Rules Engine
  • Provide timely, accurate trip context to the rules engine for template eligibility, selection, and payload construction.
  • Co-design suppression, sequencing, and failover logic informed by data quality, customer preferences, and operational realities.
  • Maintain shared observability (dashboards, alerts) for event-to-send latency, payload correctness, and incident response.
  • Architecture & Cross-Domain Integration
  • Collaborate with Customer and Flight data product owners to influence schemas, identifiers, and lifecycle events.
  • Integrate with identity resolution, consent/preference services, and eligibility engines to enable personalization and compliance.
  • Plan cutovers, deprecations, and migration paths that reduce complexity and safeguard data lineage.
  • Compliance, Risk & Controls
  • Ensure adherence to privacy, security, and regulatory requirements (e.g., CAN-SPAM/TCPA for contactability, GDPR/CCPA for data rights).
  • Steward consent, preferences, and retention policies in trip context; maintain audit trails and data governance artifacts.
  • Define incident management runbooks and escalation paths for data issues impacting communications and operations.
  • Stakeholder & Change Management
  • Align cross-functional stakeholders (Ops, Care, Digital, Marketing, Legal, Security) on priorities and release plans.
  • Lead enablement for new capabilities—training, documentation, and change communications for internal customers.
  • Facilitate UAT and incorporate feedback to improve clarity, correctness, and developer experience.
  • Delivery & Execution
  • Maintain an outcomes-focused backlog; provide clear requirements, data contracts, and acceptance criteria.
  • Plan iterative releases; measure impact with dashboards and adjust based on performance and stakeholder feedback.
  • Focus on cross-functional collaboration over ceremony compliance; unblock dependencies proactively.

What You Need To Succeed (Minimum Qualifications)

  • 5–7+ years in Product Ownership/Management with experience in data products or platforms.
  • Deep familiarity with data modeling, event-driven architectures, and data quality/governance practices.
  • Experience integrating with messaging/notification systems and consent/preference services.
  • Strong analytical skills; ability to diagnose data issues and reason about lineage, timeliness, and correctness.
  • Excellent communication and stakeholder management; able to align diverse teams on shared data contracts and outcomes.
  • Working knowledge of privacy, security, and regulatory considerations for customer communications and data handling.
  • Consistently prioritizes safety and security of self, others, and personal data.
  • Embraces diverse people, thinking, and styles.
  • Possesses a high school diploma, GED, or high school equivalency.
  • Is at least 18 years of age and has authorization to work in the United States.

What Will Give You a Competitive Edge (Preferred Qualifications)

  • Experience in travel/transportation or other real-time, operations-heavy domains.
  • Exposure to customer data platforms, identity resolution, and eligibility/personalization engines.
  • Familiarity with experimentation (A/B testing), accessibility, localization, and resiliency patterns.
  • Knowledge of cloud data ecosystems and observability tooling (logs, metrics, tracing, alerting).

Benefits And Perks To Help You Keep Climbing

Our culture is rooted in a shared dedication to living our values – Care, Integrity, Resilience, Servant Leadership, and Teamwork – every day, in everything we do. At Delta, our people are our success. At the heart of what we offer is our focus on Sharing Success with Delta employees. Exploring a career at Delta gives you a chance to see the world while earning great compensation and benefits to help you keep climbing along the way:

  • Competitive salary, industry-leading proﬁt sharing program, and performance incentives
  • 401(k) with generous company contributions up to 9%
  • Paid time off including vacation, holidays, paid personal time, maternity and parental leave
  • Comprehensive health beneﬁts including medical, dental, vision, short/long term disability and life beneﬁts
  • Family care assistance through fertility support, surrogacy and adoption assistance, lactation support, subsidized back-up care, and programs that help with loved ones in all stages
  • Holistic Wellbeing programs to support physical, emotional, social, and financial health, including access to an employee assistance program offering support for you and anyone in your household, free financial coaching, and extensive resources supporting mental health
  • Domestic and International space-available flight privileges for employees and eligible family members
  • Career development programs to achieve your long-term career goals
  • World-wide partnerships to engage in community service and innovative goals created to focus on sustainability and reducing our carbon footprint
  • Business Resource Groups created to connect employees with common interests to promote inclusion, provide perspective and help implement strategies
  • Recognition rewards and awards through the platform Unstoppable Together
  • Access to over 500 discounts, specialty savings and voluntary benefits through Deltaperks such as car and hotel rentals and auto, home, and pet insurance, legal services, and childcare

Delta Air Lines (NYSE: DAL) is the U.S. global airline leader in safety, innovation, reliability and customer experience. job.job_description:
How You'll Help Us Keep Climbing (Overview & Key Responsibilities)

The Specialist, Cargo Market Development – Americas is part of an analytical team that develops and optimizes Delta Cargo’s $1B revenue in key lanes worldwide, serving in a market development, flight steering, and performance management function for the Cargo division. This role is focused on the Americas Export line of business, covering US Export and Latin America export markets to destinations worldwide.

In partnership with Field Sales, Revenue Management, Network Planning and Operations, this position provides market development and performance management to achieve shared goals objectives.

This position reports to the Manager, Cargo Market Development and is based at Delta Cargo’s HQ in Atlanta.

Primary Functions Include

  • Coordinate actions and strategies to develop Delta’s cargo markets across the organization, including Sales, RM, Network, Sales Development, Operations, and JV Partners; identify market performance drivers and opportunities to improve Cargo revenues
  • Lead Cargo Americas export performance management, executing key elements of Cargo’s performance management cycle including weekly trends, monthly Origin Performance Reviews (OPRs), quarterly JV Performance Reviews and annual Business Review Meetings (BRMs)
  • Deliver seasonal flight steering and strategy development for 30+ seasonal lanes, including goal setting and lane performance optimization post-launch; leverage new technology to expand this flight steering coverage to the full Americas export portfolio
  • Lead implementation of new capacity share and share gap metrics to Delta Cargo worldwide; utilize to steer flights and teams to capture value from the most valuable opportunities
  • Identify the roadblocks preventing commercial development, organize cross-divisional efforts to address, track and solve
  • Enable incremental value from the Delta / LATAM Joint Venture via market development, commercial steering and performance management
  • Proactively analyze market dynamics, formulate initiatives and strategy that maximize revenues in Americas export markets
  • Utilize qualitative data to assess and value Delta’s opportunities by market, account, channel, and product
  • Lead cross functionally with local and global stakeholders to execute strategies developed and perform performance management

What You Need To Succeed (Minimum Qualifications)

  • Bachelor's degree or equivalent work experience required.
  • Minimum 3 years of related work experience.
  • Previous air cargo industry experience, including experience working with industry datasets (such as WACD and CASS).
  • Must be solution-oriented, multi-task capable and have demonstrated the ability to work well with others in a fast-paced, time-sensitive environment.
  • Possess superior financial and analytical skills, sound organizational and planning skills and a proven ability to prioritize and make sound business decisions.
  • Excellent interpersonal and collaboration skills; strong ability to seek input from and understand concerns of other stakeholders, including Field Sales, RM, Network, Operations, and Delta’s customers.
  • Demonstrates initiative, business curiosity and self-directs work.
  • Highly proficient PC skills including advanced level knowledge of Microsoft Excel, Word and PowerPoint.
  • Working knowledge of Tableau, Power BI, Hyperion or similar business intelligence tools
  • Excellent verbal and written communication skills, ability to produce leader-ready documents and presentations with limited direction.
  • Must be performing satisfactorily in current position.
  • Embraces diverse people, thinking, and styles.
  • Possesses a high school diploma, GED, or high school equivalency.
  • Is at least 18 years of age and has authorization to work in the United States.

What Will Give You a Competitive Edge (Preferred Qualifications)

  • Master’s degree: MBA or other master’s degree that enables greater analytical and business management ability
  • Advanced ability with business modeling, data analytics and data visualization tools including Tableau, Power BI, and SAS
  • Significant project management ability and experience
  • Demonstrated experience working across airline joint ventures to enable incremental value

Benefits And Perks To Help You Keep Climbing

Our culture is rooted in a shared dedication to living our values – Care, Integrity, Resilience, Servant Leadership, and Teamwork – every day, in everything we do. At Delta, our people are our success. At the heart of what we offer is our focus on Sharing Success with Delta employees. Exploring a career at Delta gives you a chance to see the world while earning great compensation and benefits to help you keep climbing along the way:

  • Competitive salary, industry-leading proﬁt sharing program, and performance incentives
  • 401(k) with generous company contributions up to 9%
  • Paid time off including vacation, holidays, paid personal time, maternity and parental leave
  • Comprehensive health beneﬁts including medical, dental, vision, short/long term disability and life beneﬁts
  • Family care assistance through fertility support, surrogacy and adoption assistance, lactation support, subsidized back-up care, and programs that help with loved ones in all stages
  • Holistic Wellbeing programs to support physical, emotional, social, and financial health, including access to an employee assistance program offering support for you and anyone in your household, free financial coaching, and extensive resources supporting mental health
  • Domestic and International space-available flight privileges for employees and eligible family members
  • Career development programs to achieve your long-term career goals
  • World-wide partnerships to engage in community service and innovative goals created to focus on sustainability and reducing our carbon footprint
  • Business Resource Groups created to connect employees with common interests to promote inclusion, provide perspective and help implement strategies
  • Recognition rewards and awards through the platform Unstoppable Together
  • Access to over 500 discounts, specialty savings and voluntary benefits through Deltaperks such as car and hotel rentals and auto, home, and pet insurance, legal services, and childcare
job.title: Specialist, Cargo Market Development – Americas

job.company_description: Choice Hotels International, Inc. (NYSE: CHH) is one of the largest lodging franchisors in the world. The challenger in upscale and a leader in midscale and extended stay, Choice® has nearly 7,500 hotels, representing more than 630,000 rooms, in 45 countries and territories. A diverse portfolio of 22 brands that range from full-service upper upscale properties to midscale, extended stay, and economy enables Choice® to meet travelers' needs in more places and for more occasions while driving more value for franchise owners and shareholders. The award-winning Choice Privileges® rewards program and co-brand credit card options provide members with a fast and easy way to earn reward nights and personalized perks. For more information, visit www.choicehotels.com. At our worldwide corporate headquarters in the Washington metropolitan area (North Bethesda, MD), our IT center in Scottsdale, AZ, and Minneapolis, MN, and through our associates across the globe, we keep those hotels humming by serving as a champion for our franchisees. When you #MakeItYourChoice, you will have the environment, the tools, and the momentum to drive your career and organizational success.
job.job_description:
Job Summary

The Senior Manager, Credit & Collections provides strategic and operational leadership for Choice Hotels’ Credit and Collections functions. This role is responsible for managing enterprise-wide credit risk, optimizing cash flow, and ensuring consistent adherence to credit policies across the organization. The Senior Manager partners closely with Finance leadership and cross-functional stakeholders to drive strong financial performance while supporting positive franchise relationships and sustainable business growth.

Responsibilities

Collections Strategy & Financial Performance

  • Analyze accounts receivable data to identify collection opportunities, leveraging advanced Excel and analytical tools to drive data-informed decisions.
  • Develop and oversee collection strategies to reduce aged receivables and improve Days Sales Outstanding (DSO).
  • Establish performance metrics and reporting to monitor portfolio health, identify risks, and track progress against targets.
  • Oversee account defaults, terminations, and the write-off process for bad debt expense.
  • Provide senior-level oversight and guidance on franchisee outreach strategies; support resolution of complex delinquency root causes while balancing risk management and franchise relationships.

Strategic Credit Leadership

  • Lead the Credit function, including credit evaluation, approval, monitoring, and ongoing risk management for franchise and corporate accounts.
  • Own and continuously enhance credit policies and procedures, ensuring alignment with business objectives, risk tolerance, and industry best practices.
  • Ensure consistent application and enforcement of credit policy across all brands and portfolios through governance, controls, and ongoing team accountability; review and approve complex or high-risk credit decisions and exceptions.
  • Proactively identify credit risk trends and recommend mitigation strategies to Finance leadership.

People Leadership & Talent Development

  • Lead, coach, and develop high-performing Credit and Collections teams through clear expectations, ongoing feedback, and performance management.
  • Manage hiring, onboarding, training, and succession planning to build a scalable and engaged team.
  • Foster a culture of accountability, continuous improvement, and collaboration.

Cross-Functional Partnership

  • Partner with Legal, Franchise Operations, Billing, and Finance to resolve complex credit and collection matters.
  • Serve as the escalation point for sensitive, high-impact, or enterprise-risk accounts, including large or multi-property licensees.
  • Communicate effectively with internal stakeholders to balance risk management with business and franchise needs.

Process Improvement & Innovation

  • Drive continuous improvement and automation across credit and collections workflows, including the evaluation and implementation of AI-enabled solutions.
  • Improve efficiency, consistency, and scalability of processes while maintaining strong internal controls.

Reporting & Executive Support

  • Prepare and deliver daily, weekly, and monthly reporting, including executive-level summaries and insights.
  • Escalate high-risk accounts and emerging trends to Finance leadership with clear, actionable recommendations.
  • Support ad hoc analyses and special projects as business needs evolve.

Qualifications

Employment Experience

  • 8+ years of progressive experience in credit, collections, accounts receivable, or financial operations.
  • 5+ years of people leadership experience managing and developing teams.

Technical Skills

  • Strong analytical skills with advanced Excel skills, including VLOOKUPs, Power Pivot, and Macros.
  • Demonstrated experience developing and enforcing credit policies and managing credit risk.
  • Proficient in Microsoft Outlook, PowerPoint, and Word.

Additional Skills & Competencies

  • Proven ability to partner cross-functionally and influence at multiple levels of the organization.
  • Demonstrated success in reducing aged receivables and improving collection outcomes.
  • Strong conflict resolution and negotiation skills.
  • Exceptional written and verbal communication abilities.

Education Requirements

  • Bachelor’s degree in Finance, Accounting, Business, or a related field or an equivalent combination of education and work experience.
  • Experience in franchising, hospitality, or multi-unit environments preferred.

WORK LOCATION:

This position is based at Choice Hotels’ Pike & Rose headquarters in North Bethesda, Maryland. The selected candidate must be able to commit to this location requirement and adhere to the company’s hybrid work schedule, which consists of four days onsite and one day remote per week.

NOTE:

This position is not eligible for relocation assistance or sponsorship.

Salary Range

The salary range for this position is $93,000 to $110,000 annually. In addition to the annual salary, this role is eligible for an annual bonus based on the terms of Choice's Management Incentive Plan (MIP).

The pay range listed is for this position and is what Choice Hotels reasonably expects to pay. We may ultimately pay more or less than the posted range, and the range may be modified in the future. An employee’s pay position within the pay range will be based on several factors including, but not limited to, relevant education, qualifications, qualifications, qualifications, certifications, experience, skills, seniority, geographic location, performance, shift, travel requirements, sales or revenue-based metrics, and business or organizational needs.

Choice prioritizes our associate wellbeing by offering a comprehensive benefits program that is both competitive and flexible to help you achieve your wellbeing goals - here are just a few:

  • Competitive compensation and benefits, including medical, dental, and vision coverage
  • Leave and paid time-off for holidays, vacation, personal, family, volunteer, sick, jury duty, bereavement, military, and religious observance
  • Financial benefits for retirement and health savings
  • Employee recognition programs
  • Discounts at Choice hotels worldwide

About Choice

Choice Hotels International, Inc. (NYSE: CHH), is one of the largest lodging franchisors in the world. With 7,500 hotels in 45+ countries and territories, we offer a range of high-quality lodging options in the upper upscale, upper midscale, midscale, extended stay, and economy segments. We’re the hotel company for those who choose to bet on themselves — the striver, the dreamer, the entrepreneur — because that’s who we are, too.

At Choice, we are united by the simple belief that tomorrow will be even better than today — for associates, our company, and our franchisees. At our worldwide corporate headquarters in North Bethesda, Maryland, at our technology center in Scottsdale, Arizona, and through our associates around the globe, every voice is heard and every idea is listened to, no matter what area of the company they come from. We are united in supporting the entrepreneurial dreams of our thousands of franchise owners, which propels us forward — giving our work at Choice a purpose larger than our business.

Our corporate office locations:

North Bethesda, MD — Located at Pike & Rose, our worldwide headquarters is less than 15 miles from Washington, D.C., one block away from the North Bethesda Metro station, with easy access to I-495, complimentary parking, electronic charging stations, restaurants and retail.

Scottsdale, AZ — Located at the northwest corner of Loop 101, the Scottsdale office is home to our technology, eCommerce and customer service organizations, with easy access to complimentary parking, electronic charging stations, restaurants and retail.

Minneapolis, MN — Select roles are based in our Minneapolis office on Highway 394, near the intersection with Highway 100, only five minutes from downtown.

Field/Remote — Select roles designated as field/remote will require associates to work from a home office, connecting virtually with Choice team members and leadership on Zoom, with possible required travel depending on the role.

Choice’s Cultural Values

Welcome and Respect Everyone | Be Bold | Be Quick | Listen | Be Curious | Show Integrity

Choice’s Leadership Principles

Act with Intention | Lead with Authenticity | Grow & Deliver
job.title: Senior Manager, Credit and Collections

job.company_description:
Delta Air Lines (NYSE: DAL) is the U.S. global airline leader in safety, innovation, reliability and customer experience. Powered by our employees around the world, Delta has for a decade led the airline industry in operational excellence while maintaining our reputation for award-winning customer service.
With our mission of connecting the people and cultures of the globe, Delta strives to foster understanding across a diverse world and serve as a force for social good.
job.job_description:
How You'll Help Us Keep Climbing (Overview & Key Responsibilities)

At Delta Air Lines, connection is at the heart of everything we do and guides our every action. We strive to welcome and care for all of our customers during their travels with us and aim to deliver an elevated experience.

We are seeking a Product Owner to lead enterprise data management and customer communications for trip experiences. This role owns the strategy and delivery of accurate, timely, and personalized notifications across push, SMS, and email. You will ensure the data that powers these communications is reliable, governed, secure, and integrated across systems—so customers receive clear information at the right moment of their journey.

Scope & Impact

  • Owns the rules engine that will drive accurate, consistent and timely notifications to our customers, interfaces closely with underlying data capabilities.
  • Operates at enterprise scale across multiple systems, channels, and business functions (e.g., Operations, Customer Care, Marketing, Legal).
  • Balances customer experience, regulatory compliance, operational realities, and technical constraints to deliver measurable outcomes.

Key Responsibilities

  • Strategy & Roadmap
  • Define a customer-centric vision and multi-quarter roadmap for trip communications and the supporting data foundation.
  • Translate outcomes into epics and features with clear success metrics and acceptance criteria.
  • Prioritize the backlog based on customer impact, risk, compliance requirements, and technical feasibility.
  • Customer Trip Communications (Omnichannel)
  • Own trip-lifecycle triggers and content across channels (push, SMS, email) including timing, frequency, and suppression logic.
  • Define personalization rules (identity, trip context, language, preferences) and guardrails (rate limiting, quiet hours, failover).
  • Coordinate with Operations and Customer Care to ensure messages reflect real-time operational realities and support resolution paths.
  • Architecture & Integration
  • Collaborate with engineering on event-driven architectures and APIs that support near real-time communications.
  • Integrate with customer data platforms and messaging providers (e.g., push gateways, SMS aggregators, email service) to ensure deliverability and observability.
  • Manage dependencies across systems (identity resolution, preferences, eligibility engines) and plan cutovers/deprecations to reduce complexity.
  • Compliance, Risk & Controls
  • Ensure adherence to applicable regulations (e.g., CAN-SPAM, TCPA, GDPR/CCPA) and internal policies.
  • Maintain audit trails, data retention practices, and escalation/runbooks for incidents affecting communications.
  • Partner with Legal, Security, and Privacy teams to review new features, templates, and data uses.
  • Stakeholder & Change Management
  • Align cross-functional stakeholders on priorities, trade-offs, and release plans; communicate status and risks clearly.
  • Lead go-to-market readiness for new communication capabilities—training, support materials, and operational runbooks.
  • Facilitate user acceptance testing and incorporate feedback to improve clarity and effectiveness.
  • Delivery & Execution
  • Maintain a transparent, outcomes-focused backlog; provide clear requirements and acceptance criteria.
  • Plan iterative releases and validate impact with data; adjust based on performance and feedback.
  • Participate in core delivery rituals as needed; focus on cross-functional collaboration over ceremony compliance.

What You Need To Succeed (Minimum Qualifications)

  • 5–7+ years in Product Ownership/Management with demonstrated experience in enterprise data or communication products.
  • Hands-on familiarity with data governance, data quality, and event-driven architectures.
  • Experience with customer messaging platforms (e.g., push, SMS, email) and preference/consent management.
  • Strong analytical skills—comfort with metrics, dashboards, and diagnosing data/communication issues.
  • Excellent written and verbal communication; ability to craft clear requirements and align diverse stakeholders.
  • Working knowledge of privacy, security, and regulatory considerations for customer communications.
  • Consistently prioritizes safety and security of self, others, and personal data.
  • Embraces diverse people, thinking, and styles.
  • Possesses a high school diploma, GED, or high school equivalency.
  • Is at least 18 years of age and has authorization to work in the United States.

What Will Give You a Competitive Edge (Preferred Qualifications)

  • Experience in travel/transportation or other real-time, operations-heavy domains.
  • Exposure to customer data platforms, identity resolution, and personalization engines.
  • Familiarity with experimentation (A/B testing), accessibility guidelines, and localization at scale.
  • Knowledge of cloud data ecosystems and observability tooling (e.g., logs, tracing, alerting).

Benefits And Perks To Help You Keep Climbing

Our culture is rooted in a shared dedication to living our values – Care, Integrity, Resilience, Servant Leadership, and Teamwork – every day, in everything we do. At Delta, our people are our success. At the heart of what we offer is our focus on Sharing Success with Delta employees. Exploring a career at Delta gives you a chance to see the world while earning great compensation and benefits to help you keep climbing along the way:

  • Competitive salary, industry-leading proﬁt sharing program, and performance incentives
  • 401(k) with generous company contributions up to 9%
  • Paid time off including vacation, holidays, paid personal time, maternity and parental leave
  • Comprehensive health beneﬁts including medical, dental, vision, short/long term disability and life beneﬁts
  • Family care assistance through fertility support, surrogacy and adoption assistance, lactation support, subsidized back-up care, and programs that help with loved ones in all stages
  • Holistic Wellbeing programs to support physical, emotional, social, and financial health, including access to an employee assistance program offering support for you and anyone in your household, free financial coaching, and extensive resources supporting mental health
  • Domestic and International space-available flight privileges for employees and eligible family members
  • Career development programs to achieve your long-term career goals
  • World-wide partnerships to engage in community service and innovative goals created to focus on sustainability and reducing our carbon footprint
  • Business Resource Groups created to connect employees with common interests to promote inclusion, provide perspective and help implement strategies
  • Recognition rewards and awards through the platform Unstoppable Together
  • Access to over 500 discounts, specialty savings and voluntary benefits through Deltaperks such as car and hotel rentals and auto, home, and pet insurance, legal services, and childcare
job.title: Product Owner, Notifications Business Rules Engine

job.company_description:
Delta Air Lines (NYSE: DAL) is the U.S. global airline leader in safety, innovation, reliability and customer experience. Powered by our employees around the world, Delta has for a decade led the airline industry in operational excellence while maintaining our reputation for award-winning customer service.
With our mission of connecting the people and cultures of the globe, Delta strives to foster understanding across a diverse world and serve as a force for social good.
job.job_description:
How You'll Help Us Keep Climbing (Overview & Key Responsibilities)

Are you motivated to join a team focused on external channel performance in support of Delta’s ability to sell its products around the world? Want to be part of implementing and optimizing efforts with our agency community, Global Distribution Systems (GDS) partners, and content distribution software providers? The Distribution team is looking for a driven individual to deliver booking platform innovation to maximize revenue within the third-party ecosystem.

The Program Manager, Distribution Operations plays a critical role in driving innovation and operational excellence across Delta’s external distribution ecosystem. This role supports strategic initiatives, manages key relationships, and collaborates cross-functionally to enhance distribution capabilities and customer experience.

In addition to being a key point of contact for our third-party distribution partners, this role involves managing relationships with Global Distribution System (GDS) partners and other third-party intermediaries. The Program Manager serves as the primary contact for GDS providers, ensuring smooth day-to-day operations, resolving break/fix issues, and supporting escalations. Through strong collaboration with cross-divisional teams such as Sales Innovation, Agency Sales Development, Revenue Management, Finance, and Digital to implement highly visible projects including the implementation of New Distribution Capability (NDC) and product merchandising.

Distribution is seeking an individual to assist internal and external stakeholders to deliver innovation and operational issue resolution within our third-party distribution partners. This position will be based in Atlanta, GA.

Key Responsibilities

  • Support development of strategies and tactics for mid-and-long term distribution innovation within the external distribution ecosystem, with specific project/initiative responsibilities such as NDC functions, new brand implementation, new route introductions, agency fraud prevention, GSA implementation, ancillary optimization, enabling enhanced core functionality and industry updates or mandates.
  • Collaborate across multiple internal and external stakeholders to drive implementation of Distribution strategies, projects and programs with our GDS partners
  • Understand and communicate data flows and technical processes across Global Distribution platforms, influencing and aligning where possible and managing the differences where not possible.
  • Serve as the primary GDS contact, managing the relationship to ensure alignment and resolution of ongoing break/fix issues, while providing daily support and handling escalations.
  • Prepare and communicate issue summaries and resolution actions to stakeholders as needed
  • Collaborate with cross-functional teams to identify and document gaps in third-party services
  • Work with distributors, intermediaries and tech providers to champion improvement in third-party booking platforms to de-commoditize customer experience, better showcase premium products, and keep pace with Delta and industry innovation.
  • Support the manager with tasks related to GDS tool development, ensuring alignment with Distribution Strategy objectives and timelines.
  • Remove roadblocks to success through strong relationships, effective communication, and creative solutions to new and long-standing challenges.
  • Partner with cross-divisional teams across Delta including Sales Innovation, Field Sales, Finance, Loyalty, Enterprise Solutions, Revenue Management, and Digital. Also, coordinate with Joint Venture airline partners to support selling of corresponding products and prevent fraudulent activity.

What You Need To Succeed (Minimum Qualifications)

  • Strong knowledge of airline distribution landscape, including strategies, programs, processes, GDS tools, and content aggregator functionality.
  • Prior experience with an airline, Global Distribution System, Travel Management Company, software or service provider to the airline industry, or content aggregator working in booking platform development.
  • Strong skills in project management, critical thinking, decision making & analytical/ problem solving skills.
  • Ability to interpret commercial and technical requirements across various audiences
  • Strong business acumen and the ability to synthesize inputs to form actionable, meaningful recommendations in support of Delta's third-party distribution strategy.
  • Demonstrates business maturity, the ability to recognize and address problems with the appropriate sense of urgency, and the capacity to work independently through ambiguity with minimal instruction or supervision.
  • Must have excellent written and oral communication skills and be able to make clear and concise presentations to internal and external stakeholders.
  • Ability to work in an autonomous environment and manage several responsibilities that demand a high degree of initiative, drive, and time management.
  • Strong interpersonal skills and the ability to effectively work with multiple teams.
  • Proficient PC skills including advanced level knowledge of Microsoft Word, Microsoft Excel, and PowerPoint.
  • High School diploma, GED or High School Equivalency and 3-5 years of experience in strategic planning, marketing/sales management, GDS or equivalent is required
  • Must be willing and able to travel occasionally and performing satisfactorily in current position.
  • Consistently prioritizes safety and security of self, others, and personal data.
  • Embraces diverse people, thinking, and styles.
  • Is at least 18 years of age and has authorization to work in the United States.

What Will Give You a Competitive Edge (Preferred Qualifications)

  • Working knowledge of Global Distribution system: SabreRed 360, Smartpoint+, Selling Platform Connect (GUI/Green screen)
  • Ability to interpret and troubleshoot airline ticketing and booking information within Global Distribution systems
  • Ability to interpret and navigate Edifact messaging
  • Bachelor’s Degree or MBA/master’s degree preferred
  • Proficient understanding of Sales processes, tools, and infrastructure

Benefits And Perks To Help You Keep Climbing

Our culture is rooted in a shared dedication to living our values – Care, Integrity, Resilience, Servant Leadership, and Teamwork – every day, in everything we do. At Delta, our people are our success. At the heart of what we offer is our focus on Sharing Success with Delta employees. Exploring a career at Delta gives you a chance to see the world while earning great compensation and benefits to help you keep climbing along the way.

Why You'll Love Delta

  • Competitive salary, industry-leading proﬁt sharing program, and performance incentives
  • 401(k) with generous company contributions up to 9%
  • Paid time off including vacation, holidays, paid personal time, maternity and parental leave
  • Comprehensive health beneﬁts including medical, dental, vision, short/long term disability and life beneﬁts
  • Family care assistance through fertility support, surrogacy and adoption assistance, lactation support, subsidized back-up care, and programs that help with loved ones in all stages
  • Holistic Wellbeing programs to support physical, emotional, social, and financial health, including access to an employee assistance program offering support for you and anyone in your household, free financial coaching, and extensive resources supporting mental health
  • Domestic and International space-available flight privileges for employees and eligible family members
  • World-wide partnerships to engage in community service and innovative goals created to focus on sustainability and reducing our carbon footprint
  • Recognition rewards and awards through the platform Unstoppable Together
  • Business Resource Groups created to connect employees with common interests to promote inclusion, provide perspective and help implement strategies
  • Access to over 500 discounts, specialty savings and voluntary benefits through Deltaperks such as car and hotel rentals and auto, home, and pet insurance, legal services, and childcare

job.company_description:
Delta Air Lines (NYSE: DAL) is the U.S. global airline leader in safety, innovation, reliability and customer experience. Powered by our employees around the world, Delta has for a decade led the airline industry in operational excellence while maintaining our reputation for award-winning customer service.
With our mission of connecting the people and cultures of the globe, Delta strives to foster understanding across a diverse world and serve as a force for social good.
job.job_description:
How You'll Help Us Keep Climbing (Overview & Key Responsibilities)

The Sales Manager for Delta’s Pharma product will be responsible for all commercial activities and business development for the North America region. This will be critical in supporting the implementation and realizing the product strategy for Pharma in alignment with the long-term strategy, growing Delta’s book of business for these products, and will report directly to the Director – Americas Sales.

Summary Of Responsibilities (not Comprehensive Of All Tasks)

  • Supports Delta Cargo’s Pharma product portfolio including HQ relationship with larger Pharma customers; builds and expands these product portfolios with a focus on speed to market, customer satisfaction, customer retention and growth of new business, so that Delta Cargo remains competitive in the industry; and provides our customers with options to support all of their shipment needs.
  • Establishes and maintains a competitive product assessment to ensure our products are at parity or better than competitive offerings for the region in collaboration with Senior Business Development Pharma
  • Drives enhancements to Pharma product portfolio to increase market share and yield with a focus on existing and new innovative solutions.
  • Develops special custom solutions for large enterprise organizations connected to the Pharma product, works through special projects in his field of responsibility and develops the annual Cargo business plan for Pharma.
  • Supports the development of new and enhance processes from documentation, solution design and training materials to ensure the successful execution of the product offering.
  • Lead and development local, regional Pharma business communities, forums, vendors and international entities within the Pharma product offering and GDP & CEIV stakeholders.
  • Represents Delta Cargo in user and industry groups to drive initiatives and standards that support our strategic objectives
  • Liaises with key areas on the passenger team to build on their experiences, identifies opportunities for synergies and generates new ideas that enhance our product portfolio. Create relationships with Passenger Sellers at key Pharma producers.
  • Researches and facilitates detailed analyses of data to determine optimum programs and goals.
  • Develops and prepares business cases and justification for Pharma growth.
  • Additional tasks may be required based on business needs.

What You Need To Succeed (Minimum Qualifications)

  • Minimum of five years of experience and understanding of cargo, pharma and healthcare industry, products, operational processes and technologies or relevant experience.
  • Must be able to influence and drive change at all levels of the organization.
  • Must have demonstrated problem solving, organizational, analytical and decision-making skills, as well as the ability to lead and direct multiple assignments simultaneously.
  • Must be able to interact effectively with executives and able to confidently speak before large groups and effectively conduct meetings.
  • Must have strong working knowledge of Microsoft Word, Excel and PowerPoint Experience; Access, as well as Tableau and other database/analytic platforms is preferred.
  • Should possess excellent oral and written communication skills and have a professional demeanor.
  • Must have effective time management and multi-tasking skills in addition to critical thinking skills and high emotional intelligence.
  • Must be able to work well under pressure and effectively balance multiple, rapidly shifting priorities with little direct supervision.
  • Must be able to travel (up to 60%) and represent Delta at industry / customer functions.
  • Must be a team player.
  • Must be performing satisfactorily in present position.
  • Consistently prioritizes safety and security of self, others, and personal data.
  • Embraces diverse people, thinking, and styles.
  • Possesses a high school diploma, GED, or high school equivalency.
  • Is at least 18 years of age and has authorization to work in the United States.

What Will Give You a Competitive Edge (Preferred Qualifications)

  • A bachelor’s degree in business, marketing, supply-chain or finance. An MBA is preferred.
  • Aviation or transportation industry experience.
  • Cargo sales experience or similar industries.

Benefits And Perks To Help You Keep Climbing

  • Our culture is rooted in a shared dedication to living our values – Care, Integrity, Resilience and Servant Leadership – every day, in everything we do. At Delta, our people are our success. At the heart of what we offer is our focus on Sharing Success with Delta employees. Exploring a career at Delta gives you a chance to see the world while earning great compensation and benefits to help you keep climbing along the way:
  • Competitive salary, industry-leading proﬁt sharing program, and performance incentives.
  • 401(k) with generous company contributions up to 9%
  • New hires are eligible for up to 2-weeks of vacation. This is earned for use in the following vacation year (April 1 – March 31).
  • In addition to vacation, new hires are eligible for up to 56 hours of paid personal time within a 12-month period
    • 10 paid holidays per calendar year
  • Birthing parents are eligible for 12-weeks of paid maternity/parental leave.
  • Non-birthing parents are eligible for 2-weeks of paid parental leave.
  • Comprehensive health beneﬁts including medical, dental, vision, short/long term disability and life insurance beneﬁts.
  • Family care assistance through fertility support, surrogacy and adoption assistance, lactation support, subsidized back-up care, and programs that help with loved ones in all stages.
  • Holistic Wellbeing programs to support physical, emotional, social, and financial health, including access to an employee assistance program offering support for you and anyone in your household, free financial coaching, and extensive resources supporting mental health.
  • Domestic and International space-available flight privileges for employees and eligible family members.
  • Career development programs to achieve your long-term career goals.
  • World-wide partnerships to engage in community service and innovative goals created to focus on sustainability and reducing our carbon footprint.
  • Business Resource Groups created to connect employees with common interests to promote inclusion, provide perspective and help implement strategies.
  • Recognition rewards and awards through the platform Unstoppable Together.
  • Access to over 500 discounts, specialty savings and voluntary benefits through Deltaperks such as car and hotel rentals and auto, home, and pet insurance, legal services, and childcare.
job.title: Senior Manager, Pharma Sales - Cargo

job.company_description:
Delta Air Lines (NYSE: DAL) is the U.S. global airline leader in safety, innovation, reliability and customer experience. Powered by our employees around the world, Delta has for a decade led the airline industry in operational excellence while maintaining our reputation for award-winning customer service.
With our mission of connecting the people and cultures of the globe, Delta strives to foster understanding across a diverse world and serve as a force for social good.
job.job_description:
How You'll Help Us Keep Climbing (Overview & Key Responsibilities)

At Delta Air Lines, connection is at the heart of everything we do and guides our every action. We strive to welcome and care for all of our customers during their travels with us and aim to deliver an elevated experience.

Delta is on a journey to become a world-class retailer, changing the way Delta can sell and service flight and ancillary products and improve the customer experience. To achieve this goal, Delta’s Retail Strategy is to consistently offer, sell, and service high quality personalized products in the customer’s channel of choice. Enterprise Solutions (ES) is seeking an Ancillary Order Management Product Manager to lead the vision and execution of IT development roadmaps that enable ordering, fulfilling, and servicing ancillary products such as seats, bags, wifi, catering and more.

The Order Management team is responsible for the following scope:

  • Orchestration to create, retrieve, change and cancel an order
  • Create and update the order record
  • Creation of records in legacy systems of record

We are looking for candidates who exhibit curiosity, are motivated to solve challenging problems, and can think creatively to identify solutions.

Responsibilities

  • Develop a business vision and a sequenced roadmap for the order management of ancillary products and services
  • Partner with stakeholders across the enterprise to determine requirements for the order management of ancillary products and services
  • Collaborate with Ancillary Offer business and IT partners to translate upstream business requirements into usable and reliable solutions that span the full retailing process from Offer to Order C Servicing.
  • Lead agile development teams to implement requirements.
  • Collaborate with other agile development teams, both upstream and downstream, to identify dependencies required to deliver the order management roadmap.
  • Communicate progress against development roadmap to leaders and stakeholders.
  • Learn the business rationale behind ancillary order data schemas and orchestration patterns with the goal of building solutions that are product-agnostic, channel-agnostic, and form of payment-agnostic

What You Need To Succeed (Minimum Qualifications)

  • Proven success in understanding and managing complex processes.
  • Able to demonstrate intellectual curiosity and critical thinking
  • Experience translating a business vision into written technical requirements.
  • Ability to communicate clearly to business leaders and technical developers.
  • Must have the ability to work well with diverse groups in a time sensitive and team environment with minimal supervision.
  • Results oriented and capable of meeting or exceeding deadlines with attention to detail and follow through.
  • Consistently prioritizes safety and security of self, others, and personal data.
  • Embraces diverse people, thinking, and styles.
  • Possesses a high school diploma, GED, or high school equivalency.
  • Is at least 18 years of age and has authorization to work in the United States.

What Will Give You a Competitive Edge (Preferred Qualifications)

  • Bachelor's Degree with at least 5+ years of relevant work experience
  • Preferred Major in quantitative discipline such as Management Information Systems, Business Administration, Economics, Finance, or Engineering.
  • Combination of at least 5+ years of relevant working experience in Revenue Management, Internal or External channels, Business Intelligence, and Data Analytics.
  • Experience in at least one commercial or operations function such as Reservations, ACS, eCommerce, Revenue Management, Sales, or IT preferred.
  • Experience working in an Agile environment as a Product Manager/Owner.

Benefits And Perks To Help You Keep Climbing

Our culture is rooted in a shared dedication to living our values – Care, Integrity, Resilience, Servant Leadership, and Teamwork – every day, in everything we do. At Delta, our people are our success. At the heart of what we offer is our focus on Sharing Success with Delta employees. Exploring a career at Delta gives you a chance to see the world while earning great compensation and benefits to help you keep climbing along the way:

  • Competitive salary, industry-leading proﬁt sharing program, and performance incentives
  • 401(k) with generous company contributions up to 9%
  • Paid time off including vacation, holidays, paid personal time, maternity and parental leave
  • Comprehensive health beneﬁts including medical, dental, vision, short/long term disability and life beneﬁts
  • Family care assistance through fertility support, surrogacy and adoption assistance, lactation support, subsidized back-up care, and programs that help with loved ones in all stages
  • Holistic Wellbeing programs to support physical, emotional, social, and financial health, including access to an employee assistance program offering support for you and anyone in your household, free financial coaching, and extensive resources supporting mental health
  • Domestic and International space-available flight privileges for employees and eligible family members
  • Career development programs to achieve your long-term career goals
  • World-wide partnerships to engage in community service and innovative goals created to focus on sustainability and reducing our carbon footprint
  • Business Resource Groups created to connect employees with common interests to promote inclusion, provide perspective and help implement strategies
  • Recognition rewards and awards through the platform Unstoppable Together
  • Access to over 500 discounts, specialty savings and voluntary benefits through Deltaperks such as car and hotel rentals and auto, home, and pet insurance, legal services, and childcare

job.job_description:
JOB SUMMARY:

Choice Hotels, one of the world’s largest lodging franchisors, is seeking a Brand Manager, Upper Midscale Brands to develop and execute holistic strategies for our flagship portfolio, including Comfort Inn & Suites and Country Inn & Suites. This individual contributor role will focus on driving unit growth, revenue, franchisee profitability, and enhancing guest experiences. The Brand Manager will define and position the brands within their segment and activate strategies across consumer touchpoints

KEY RESPONSIBILITIES:

Brand Strategy

  • Develop and implement strategies that strengthen the franchisee, consumer, and developer value proposition for Comfort and Country Inn & Suites.
  • Act as a cross-functional connector, integrating insights and business needs to deliver cohesive brand strategies.
  • Create data-driven reports and compelling business cases for internal and external audiences.
  • Continuously evaluate and refine the brand’s economic model to maintain competitive advantage.
  • Collaborate with leadership and cross-functional teams to identify trends, optimize performance, and measure success.
  • Champion distinctive guest experiences through brand hallmarks, amenities, and service touchpoints.
  • Monitor market trends and competitive dynamics to recommend proactive strategies.

Project & Program Management

  • Develop and oversee project plans with milestones, deliverables, and performance metrics.
  • Monitor progress, identify process gaps, and communicate updates to stakeholders.
  • Build strong relationships across the organization to drive collaboration and achieve objectives.
  • Manage budgets for owner meetings, brand events, and Choice Hotels’ annual Convention.

Strategic Brand Champion

  • Lead ongoing development and management of brand programs and standards.
  • Represent Choice Hotels and the brands at industry events and conferences.
  • Advocate for the brand during key engagements with franchisees and developers.
  • Collaborate across segments to ensure consistency and efficiency within the Brand organization.

QUALIFICATIONS:

Education

  • Bachelor’s degree in Marketing, Business Administration, Hospitality Management, or related field or equivalent combination of education and work experience.

Experience

  • 5–7 years of progressive experience in brand management, marketing strategy, or hospitality operations.
  • Experience in franchise or multi-unit business models strongly preferred.
  • Proven ability to manage brand initiatives from concept to execution with measurable impact.

Technical Skills & Competencies

  • Strong understanding of hospitality, consumer insights, and midscale market dynamics.
  • Strategic thinking and analytical skills with ability to translate insights into actionable plans.
  • Excellent project management and organizational skills; adept at managing multiple priorities.
  • Exceptional communication and storytelling skills to influence diverse stakeholders.
  • Collaborative mindset with ability to build strong internal and external relationships.
  • Financial acumen for evaluating ROI, budgets, and performance metrics.
  • High proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite; familiarity with data visualization and analytics tools a plus.
  • Experience leveraging AI tools for efficiency and workload management.

SALARY RANGE:

The salary range for this position is $98,088 to $115,000 annually. In addition to the annual salary, this role is eligible for an annual bonus based on the terms of Choice's Management Incentive Plan (MIP).

The pay range listed is for this position and is what Choice Hotels reasonably expects to pay. We may ultimately pay more or less than the posted range, and the range may be modified in the future. An employee’s pay position within the pay range will be based on several factors including, but not limited to, relevant education, qualifications, certifications, experience, skills, seniority, geographic location, performance, shift, travel requirements, sales or revenue-based metrics, and business or organizational needs.

WORK LOCATION:

This position is based at Choice Hotels’ Pike & Rose headquarters in North Bethesda, Maryland. The selected candidate must be able to commit to this location requirement and adhere to the company’s hybrid work schedule, which consists of four days onsite and one day remote per week.

NOTE:

This position is not eligible for relocation assistance or sponsorship.

Choice prioritizes our associate wellbeing by offering a comprehensive benefits program that is both competitive and flexible to help you achieve your wellbeing goals - here are just a few:

  • Competitive compensation and benefits, including medical, dental, and vision coverage
  • Leave and paid time-off for holidays, vacation, personal, family, volunteer, sick, jury duty, bereavement, military, and religious observance
  • Financial benefits for retirement and health savings
  • Employee recognition programs
  • Discounts at Choice hotels worldwide

About Choice

Choice Hotels International, Inc. (NYSE: CHH), is one of the largest lodging franchisors in the world. With 7,500 hotels in 45+ countries and territories, we offer a range of high-quality lodging options in the upper upscale, upper midscale, midscale, extended stay, and economy segments. We’re the hotel company for those who choose to bet on themselves — the striver, the dreamer, the entrepreneur — because that’s who we are, too.

At Choice, we are united by the simple belief that tomorrow will be even better than today — for associates, our company, and our franchisees. At our worldwide corporate headquarters in North Bethesda, Maryland, at our technology center in Scottsdale, Arizona, and through our associates around the globe, every voice is heard and every idea is listened to, no matter what area of the company they come from. We are united in supporting the entrepreneurial dreams of our thousands of franchise owners, which propels us forward — giving our work at Choice a purpose larger than our business.

Our corporate office locations:

North Bethesda, MD — Located at Pike & Rose, our worldwide headquarters is less than 15 miles from Washington, D.C., one block away from the North Bethesda Metro station, with easy access to I-495, complimentary parking, electronic charging stations, restaurants and retail.

Scottsdale, AZ — Located at the northwest corner of Loop 101, the Scottsdale office is home to our technology, eCommerce and customer service organizations, with easy access to complimentary parking, electronic charging stations, restaurants and retail.

Minneapolis, MN — Select roles are based in our Minneapolis office on Highway 394, near the intersection with Highway 100, only five minutes from downtown.

Field/Remote — Select roles designated as field/remote will require associates to work from a home office, connecting virtually with Choice team members and leadership on Zoom, with possible required travel depending on the role.

Choice’s Cultural Values

Welcome and Respect Everyone | Be Bold | Be Quick | Listen | Be Curious | Show Integrity

Choice’s Leadership Principles

Act with Intention | Lead with Authenticity | Grow & Deliver
